revision.comment: [[WP:AES|←]]新しいページ: '{{神社 |名称=須波麻神社 |所在地=大阪府大東市中垣内 2-11 |緯度度= |地図国コード = JP |祭神=[[大国主命]] |社格=旧郷社 |...'
revision.text:
{{神社 |名称=須波麻神社 |所在地=大阪府大東市中垣内 2-11 |緯度度= |地図国コード = JP |祭神=[[大国主命]] |社格=旧郷社 |創建= |本殿=春日造 |例祭=１０月１６日より３日間 |}} '''須波麻神社'''は[[大阪府]][[大東市]]中垣内にある[[神社]]。 == 歴史 == 言い伝えによると古くは[[出雲大社]]と同体で10月7日に大祭をしていたという。創建の詳細は定かではないが、平安時代の「延喜式」「神名帳」には既に記載されており、市内で唯一の[[式内社]]である。讃良郡から若江郡に至るまで、数十ヶ村の氏子をかかえていた。明治６年に讃良郡内十一ヶ村の付属[[郷社]]になり、明治40年9月には寺川の[[大谷神社]]、龍間の[[龍間神社]]、深野の[[坐間神社]]の三社が合祀されたこともあった。現在は中垣内の[[産土神]]である。
境内には合計十一基の燈籠があるが、境内入り口燈篭（1830年文政13年）は道しるべを兼ねたもので、近くの[[東高野街道]]筋から移したものである。 ;社名について この神社がある地域は昔[[河内湾]]→[[河内湖]]が存在し、それが更に縮小していくことで徐々に集落等がつくられていったが、なおも[[深開池]]や[[深野池]]など大きな池として存在が残った。この神社の西はそうした池の水辺だったとされ、その水辺に祀られた神として渚濱(すはま)に位置していたことから、現在の社名になったのではないかと推測されている。ちなみにこの「渚浜(すはま)」という地名は大東市において知りうる最古の地名である。 ==関連== [[産土神]] == 外部リンク == *[http://www.geocities.jp/engishiki01/kawachi/html/040701-01.html 須波麻神社] *[http://www.xhotzone.net/vh/vh08101901.php 大東市中垣内 須波摩神社] {{デフォルトソート:すはまじんじゃ}} [[Category:大阪府の神社]] [[Category:大阪府の旧郷社]] [[Category:大東市]] {{Shinto-stub}} {{Pref-stub|pref=大阪府}}

{{神社 |名称=須波麻神社 |所在地=大阪府大東市中垣内 2-11 |緯度度= |地図国コード = JP |祭神=[[大国主命]] |社格=旧郷社 |創建= |本殿=銅板葺春日造 |例祭=１０月１６日より３日間 |}} '''須波麻神社'''は[[大阪府]][[大東市]]中垣内にある[[神社]]。 == 歴史 == 言い伝えによると古くは[[出雲大社]]と同体で10月7日に大祭をしていたという。創建の詳細は定かではないが、平安時代の「延喜式」「神名帳」には既に記載されており、市内で唯一の[[式内社]]である。讃良郡から若江郡に至るまで、数十ヶ村の氏子をかかえていた。明治６年に讃良郡内十一ヶ村の付属[[郷社]]になり、明治40年9月には寺川の[[大谷神社]]、龍間の[[龍間神社]]、深野の[[坐間神社]]の三社が合祀されたこともあった。現在は中垣内の[[産土神]]である。
境内には合計十一基の燈籠があるが、境内入り口燈篭（1830年文政13年）は道しるべを兼ねたもので、近くの[[東高野街道]]筋から移したものである。 ;社名について この神社がある地域は昔[[河内湾]]→[[河内湖]]が存在し、それが更に縮小していくことで徐々に集落等がつくられていったが、なおも[[深開池]]や[[深野池]]など大きな池として存在が残った。この神社の西はそうした池の水辺であったとされ、当初は[[大国主命]]ではなく別の水難除けの水神を祀った神社だったという説があり、 現在の社名は「渚浜／洲浜(すはま)」（つまり水辺）から転じたのではないかと推測されている。
ちなみにこの「渚浜(すはま)」という地名は大東市において知りうる最古の地名である。 ==関連== [[産土神]] ==参考図書== *古社紀行（摂津・河内・和泉）／國井義典 == 外部リンク == *[http://www.geocities.jp/engishiki01/kawachi/html/040701-01.html 須波麻神社] *[http://www.xhotzone.net/vh/vh08101901.php 大東市中垣内 須波摩神社] {{デフォルトソート:すはまじんじゃ}} [[Category:大阪府の神社]] [[Category:大阪府の旧郷社]] [[Category:大東市]] {{Shinto-stub}} {{Pref-stub|pref=大阪府}}

revision.comment: 座標
revision.text:
{{神社 |画像 = [[画像:須波摩神社.jpg|250px]] |名称 = 須波麻神社 |所在地 = [[大阪府]][[大東市]]中垣内2-11 |位置 = {{ウィキ座標2段度分秒|34|42|15.8|N|135|38|59.7|E|region:JP}} |祭神 = [[大国主命]] |社格 = 式内社（小）・郷社 |創建 = |本殿 = 銅板葺春日造 |例祭 = 10月16日より3日間 }} '''須波麻神社'''は、[[大阪府]][[大東市]]中垣内にある[[神社]]である。 == 歴史 == 言い伝えによると古くは[[出雲大社]]と同体で10月7日に大祭をしていたという。創建の詳細は定かではないが、平安時代の「延喜式」「神名帳」には既に記載されており、市内で唯一の[[式内社]]である。讃良郡から若江郡に至るまで、数十ヶ村の氏子をかかえていた。明治６年に讃良郡内十一ヶ村の付属[[郷社]]になり、明治40年9月には寺川の[[大谷神社]]、龍間の[[龍間神社]]、深野の[[坐間神社]]の三社が合祀されたこともあった。現在は中垣内の[[産土神]]である。
境内には合計十一基の燈籠があるが、境内入り口燈篭（1830年文政13年）は道しるべを兼ねたもので、近くの[[東高野街道]]筋から移したものである。 ;社名について この神社がある地域は昔[[河内湾]]が存在し、それが[[河内潟]]、[[河内湖]]と[[淀川]]や[[大和川]]などの土砂の流入によって縮小していくことで徐々に集落等がつくられていったが、なおも[[深開池]]や[[深野池]]など大きな池として存在が残った。この神社の西はそうした池の水辺であったとされ、当初は[[大国主命]]ではなく別の水難除けの水神を祀った神社だったという説があり、 現在の社名は「渚浜／洲浜(すはま)」（つまり水辺）から転じたのではないかと推測されている。
ちなみにこの「渚浜(すはま)」という地名は大東市において知りうる最古の地名である。 == 関連 == [[産土神]] == 参考文献 == * 古社紀行（摂津・河内・和泉）／國井義典 == 外部リンク == * [http://www.geocities.jp/engishiki01/kawachi/html/040701-01.html 須波麻神社] * [http://www.xhotzone.net/vh/vh08101901.php 大東市中垣内 須波摩神社] {{Shinto-stub}} {{Pref-stub|pref=大阪府}} {{デフォルトソート:すはましんしや}} [[Category:大阪府の神社]] [[Category:大阪府の旧郷社]] [[Category:大東市]]

revision.text:
{{神社 |名称=須波麻神社 |画像=[[画像:須波摩神社.jpg|250px]]
境内遠景 |所在地=[[大阪府]][[大東市]]中垣内2-11 | 緯度度 = 34 | 緯度分 = 42 | 緯度秒 = 15.8 | N(北緯)及びS(南緯) = N | 経度度 = 135 |経度分 = 38 | 経度秒 = 59.7 | E(東経)及びW(西経) = E | 地図国コード = JP | 地図 = Japan Osaka |祭神=[[大国主命]] |社格=[[式内社]]（小）
旧[[郷社]] |創建=不詳 |本殿= |別名= |札所等= |例祭= |神事= }} '''須波麻神社'''（すはまじんじゃ）は、[[大阪府]][[大東市]]中垣内にある[[神社]]。[[式内社]]で、[[旧社格]]は[[郷社]]。 == 歴史 == 言い伝えによると古くは[[出雲大社]]と同体で10月7日に大祭をしていたという。創建の詳細は定かではないが、平安時代の「[[延喜式神名帳]]」には既に記載されており、大東市内で唯一の[[式内社]]である。[[讃良郡]]から若江郡に至るまで、数十ヶ村の氏子をかかえていた。1873年（明治6年）に讃良郡内十一ヶ村の[[郷社]]になり、1907年（明治40年）9月には寺川の[[大谷神社]]、龍間の[[龍間神社]]、深野の[[坐間神社]]の三社が合祀されたこともあった。現在は中垣内の[[産土神]]である。 ;社名について この神社がある地域は昔[[河内湾]]が存在し、それが[[河内潟]]、[[河内湖]]と[[淀川]]や[[大和川]]などの土砂の流入によって縮小していくことで徐々に集落等がつくられていったが、なおも[[深開池]]や[[深野池]]など大きな池として存在が残った。この神社の西はそうした池の水辺であったとされ、当初は[[大国主命]]ではなく別の水難除けの水神を祀った神社だったという説があり、現在の社名は「渚浜／洲浜(すはま)」（つまり水辺）から転じたのではないかと推測されている。ちなみにこの「渚浜(すはま)」という地名は大東市において知りうる最古の地名である。 == 境内 == 境内には合計十一基の燈籠があるが、1830年（文政13年）製の境内入り口燈篭は道しるべを兼ねたもので、近くの[[東高野街道]]筋から移したものである。 == 現地情報 == ;所在地 *[[大阪府]][[大東市]]中垣内2-11 ;交通アクセス * 最寄駅：JR[[学研都市線]][[野崎駅 (大阪府)|野崎駅]]下車後、徒歩約28分（南東へ約2.2km） == 参考文献 == * 古社紀行（摂津・河内・和泉）／國井義典 == 関連項目 == *[[河内国の式内社一覧]] == 外部リンク == * [http://www.geocities.jp/engisiki/kawachi/html/040701-01.html　須波麻神社] {{Shinto-stub}} {{神道 横}} {{デフォルトソート:すはましんしや}} [[Category:大阪府の神社]] [[Category:大阪府の旧郷社]] [[Category:河内国の式内社]] [[Category:単立神社]] [[Category:大東市の建築物]] [[Category:大東市の歴史]]

{{神社 |名称=須波麻神社 |画像=[[画像:須波摩神社.jpg|250px]]
境内遠景 |所在地=[[大阪府]][[大東市]]中垣内2-11 | 緯度度 = 34 | 緯度分 = 42 | 緯度秒 = 15.8 | N(北緯)及びS(南緯) = N | 経度度 = 135 |経度分 = 38 | 経度秒 = 59.7 | E(東経)及びW(西経) = E | 地図国コード = JP | 地図 = Japan Osaka |祭神=[[大国主命]] |社格=[[式内社]]（小）
旧[[郷社]] |創建=不詳 |本殿= |別名= |札所等= |例祭= |神事= }} '''須波麻神社'''（すはまじんじゃ）は、[[大阪府]][[大東市]]中垣内にある[[神社]]。[[式内社]]で、[[旧社格]]は[[郷社]]。 == 歴史 == [[ファイル:須波麻神社のクロガネモチ.jpg|サムネイル|境内のクロガネモチ（保存樹木）]] 言い伝えによると古くは[[出雲大社]]と同体で10月7日に大祭をしていたという。創建の詳細は定かではないが、平安時代の「[[延喜式神名帳]]」には既に記載されており、大東市内で唯一の[[式内社]]である。[[讃良郡]]から若江郡に至るまで、数十ヶ村の氏子をかかえていた。1873年（明治6年）に讃良郡内十一ヶ村の[[郷社]]になり、1907年（明治40年）9月には寺川の[[大谷神社]]、龍間の[[龍間神社]]、深野の[[坐間神社]]の三社が合祀されたこともあった。現在は中垣内の[[産土神]]である。 ;社名について この神社がある地域は昔[[河内湾]]が存在し、それが[[河内潟]]、[[河内湖]]と[[淀川]]や[[大和川]]などの土砂の流入によって縮小していくことで徐々に集落等がつくられていったが、なおも[[深開池]]や[[深野池]]など大きな池として存在が残った。この神社の西はそうした池の水辺であったとされ、当初は[[大国主命]]ではなく別の水難除けの水神を祀った神社だったという説があり、現在の社名は「渚浜／洲浜(すはま)」（つまり水辺）から転じたのではないかと推測されている。ちなみにこの「渚浜(すはま)」という地名は大東市において知りうる最古の地名である。 == 境内 == [[ファイル:須波麻神社の石灯籠（併用道標）.jpg|サムネイル|石灯籠（併用道標）]] 社殿手前には[[クロガネモチ]]の大木が自生しており、大東市の保存樹木に指定されている。 境内には合計十一基の燈籠があるが、1830年（文政13年）製の境内入り口に置かれた燈篭は道しるべを兼ねたもので、近くの[[東高野街道]]筋から移したものである。 == 現地情報 == ;所在地 *[[大阪府]][[大東市]]中垣内2-11 ;交通アクセス * 最寄駅：JR[[学研都市線]][[野崎駅 (大阪府)|野崎駅]]下車後、徒歩約28分（南東へ約2.2km） * [[近鉄バス]]四條畷線中垣内バス停下車後、徒歩約7分 == 参考文献 == * 古社紀行（摂津・河内・和泉）／國井義典 == 関連項目 == *[[河内国の式内社一覧]] == 外部リンク == * [https://web.archive.org/web/20181001135435/http://www.geocities.jp/engisiki/kawachi/html/040701-01.html　須波麻神社] {{Shinto-stub}} {{神道 横}} {{デフォルトソート:すはましんしや}} [[Category:大阪府の神社]] [[Category:大阪府の旧郷社]] [[Category:河内国の式内社]] [[Category:単立神社]] [[Category:大東市の建築物]] [[Category:大東市の歴史]]

{{nihongo|'''Suhama Shrine (Daito, Osaka)'''|須波麻神社}} is a [[shinto shrine]] in the [[Engishiki Jinmyōchō]]. It is located in [[Kawachi Province]].

{{nihongo|'''Suhama Shrine (Daito, Osaka)'''|須波麻神社}} is a [[shinto shrine]] in the [[Engishiki Jinmyōchō]]. It is located in [[Kawachi Province]].

{{nihongo|'''Suhama Shrine (Daito, Osaka)'''|須波麻神社}} is a [[shinto shrine]] in the [[Engishiki Jinmyōchō]]. It is located in [[Kawachi Province]].

{{nihongo|'''Suhama Shrine (Daito, Osaka)'''|須波麻神社}} is a [[shinto shrine]] in the [[Engishiki Jinmyōchō]]. It is located in [[Kawachi Province]].

{{nihongo|'''Suhama Shrine (Daito, Osaka)'''|須波麻神社}} is a [[shinto shrine]] in the [[Engishiki Jinmyōchō]]. It is located in [[Kawachi Province]].

{{nihongo|'''Suhama Shrine (Daito, Osaka)'''|須波麻神社}} is a [[shinto shrine]] in the [[Engishiki Jinmyōchō]]. It is located in [[Kawachi Province]].

{{nihongo|'''Suhama Shrine (Daito, Osaka)'''|須波麻神社}} is a [[shinto shrine]] in the [[Engishiki Jinmyōchō]]. It is located in [[Kawachi Province]].

{{nihongo|'''Suhama Shrine (Daito, Osaka)'''|須波麻神社}} is a [[shinto shrine]] in the [[Engishiki Jinmyōchō]]. It is located in [[Kawachi Province]].

{{nihongo|'''Suhama Shrine (Daito, Osaka)'''|須波麻神社}} is a [[shinto shrine]] in the [[Engishiki Jinmyōchō]]. It is located in [[Kawachi Province]].

{{nihongo|'''Suhama Shrine (Daito, Osaka)'''|須波麻神社}} is a [[shinto shrine]] in the [[Engishiki Jinmyōchō]]. It is located in [[Kawachi Province]].

{{nihongo|'''Suhama Shrine (Daito, Osaka)'''|須波麻神社}} is a [[shinto shrine]] in the [[Engishiki Jinmyōchō]]. It is located in [[Kawachi Province]].

{{神社 |名称=須波麻神社 |画像=[[画像:須波摩神社.jpg|250px]]
境内遠景 |所在地=[[大阪府]][[大東市]]中垣内2-11 |位置= {{WikidataCoord|display=inline,title}} |祭神=[[大国主命]] |社格=[[式内社]]（小）
旧[[郷社]] |創建=不詳 |本殿= |別名= |札所等= |例祭= |神事= |地図 = {{Location map|Japan Osaka|label=須波麻神社|position=bottom|width=200|mark=Shinto torii icon vermillion.svg|marksize=15|float=center|caption=}} {{maplink2|frame=yes|plain=yes|type=point|zoom=12|marker=religious-shinto|frame-align=center|frame-width=200|frame-height=200}} }} '''須波麻神社'''（すはまじんじゃ）は、[[大阪府]][[大東市]]中垣内にある[[神社]]。[[式内社]]で、[[旧社格]]は[[郷社]]。 == 歴史 == [[ファイル:須波麻神社のクロガネモチ.jpg|サムネイル|境内の[[クロガネモチ]]（[[保存樹木]]）]] 言い伝えによると古くは[[出雲大社]]と同体で10月7日に大祭をしていたという。創建の詳細は定かではないが、平安時代の「[[延喜式神名帳]]」には既に記載されており、大東市内で唯一の[[式内社]]である。[[讃良郡]]から[[若江郡]]に至るまで、数十ヶ村の氏子をかかえていた。1873年（明治6年）に讃良郡内十一ヶ村の[[郷社]]になり、1907年（明治40年）9月には寺川の[[大谷神社]]、龍間の[[龍間神社]]、深野の[[坐間神社]]の三社が合祀されたこともあった。現在は中垣内の[[産土神]]である。 ;社名について この神社がある地域は昔[[河内湾]]が存在し、それが[[河内潟]]、[[河内湖]]と[[淀川]]や[[大和川]]などの土砂の流入によって縮小していくことで徐々に集落等がつくられていったが、なおも[[深開池]]や[[深野池]]など大きな池として存在が残った。この神社の西はそうした池の水辺であったとされ、当初は[[大国主命]]ではなく別の水難除けの水神を祀った神社だったという説があり、現在の社名は「渚浜／洲浜(すはま)」（つまり水辺）から転じたのではないかと推測されている。ちなみにこの「渚浜(すはま)」という地名は大東市において知りうる最古の地名である。 == 境内 == [[ファイル:須波麻神社の石灯籠（併用道標）.jpg|サムネイル|[[灯籠|石灯籠]]（併用[[道標]]）]] 社殿手前にはクロガネモチの大木が自生しており、大東市の保存樹木に指定されている。 境内には合計十一基の灯籠があるが、1830年（文政13年）製の境内入り口に置かれた灯籠は道しるべを兼ねたもので、近くの[[東高野街道]]筋から移したものである。 == 現地情報 == ;所在地 *[[大阪府]][[大東市]]中垣内2-11 ;交通アクセス * 最寄駅：JR[[学研都市線]][[野崎駅 (大阪府)|野崎駅]]下車後、徒歩約28分（南東へ約2.2km） * [[近鉄バス]]四條畷線中垣内バス停下車後、徒歩約7分 == 参考文献 == * 古社紀行（摂津・河内・和泉）／國井義典 == 関連項目 == *[[河内国の式内社一覧]] == 外部リンク == * [https://web.archive.org/web/20181001135435/http://www.geocities.jp/engisiki/kawachi/html/040701-01.html　須波麻神社] {{Shinto-stub}} {{神道 横}} {{デフォルトソート:すはましんしや}} [[Category:大阪府の神社]] [[Category:大阪府の旧郷社]] [[Category:河内国の式内社]] [[Category:単立神社]] [[Category:大東市の建築物]] [[Category:大東市の歴史]]

[[Category:Shikinai Shōsha]] [[Category:Shrines receiving Hoe and Quiver]] [[Category:Kanpei-sha]] {{translated page|ja|須波麻神社|version=107258089|comment=Imported full ja history}}[[Category:Automerged Japanese text]] == Japanese Wikipedia content == {{神社 |名称=須波麻神社 |画像=[[画像:須波摩神社.jpg|250px]]
境内遠景 |所在地=[[大阪府]][[大東市]]中垣内2-11 |位置= {{WikidataCoord|display=inline,title}} |祭神=[[大国主命]] |社格=[[式内社]]（小）
旧[[郷社]] |創建=不詳 |本殿= |別名= |札所等= |例祭= |神事= |地図 = {{Location map|Japan Osaka|label=須波麻神社|position=bottom|width=200|mark=Shinto torii icon vermillion.svg|marksize=15|float=center|caption=}} {{maplink2|frame=yes|plain=yes|type=point|zoom=12|marker=religious-shinto|frame-align=center|frame-width=200|frame-height=200}} }} '''須波麻神社'''（すはまじんじゃ）は、[[大阪府]][[大東市]]中垣内にある[[神社]]。[[式内社]]で、[[旧社格]]は[[郷社]]。 == 歴史 == [[ファイル:須波麻神社のクロガネモチ.jpg|サムネイル|境内の[[クロガネモチ]]（[[保存樹木]]）]] 言い伝えによると古くは[[出雲大社]]と同体で10月7日に大祭をしていたという。創建の詳細は定かではないが、平安時代の「[[延喜式神名帳]]」には既に記載されており、大東市内で唯一の[[式内社]]である。[[讃良郡]]から[[若江郡]]に至るまで、数十ヶ村の氏子をかかえていた。1873年（明治6年）に讃良郡内十一ヶ村の[[郷社]]になり、1907年（明治40年）9月には寺川の[[大谷神社]]、龍間の[[龍間神社]]、深野の[[坐間神社]]の三社が合祀されたこともあった。現在は中垣内の[[産土神]]である。 ;社名について この神社がある地域は昔[[河内湾]]が存在し、それが[[河内潟]]、[[河内湖]]と[[淀川]]や[[大和川]]などの土砂の流入によって縮小していくことで徐々に集落等がつくられていったが、なおも[[深開池]]や[[深野池]]など大きな池として存在が残った。この神社の西はそうした池の水辺であったとされ、当初は[[大国主命]]ではなく別の水難除けの水神を祀った神社だったという説があり、現在の社名は「渚浜／洲浜(すはま)」（つまり水辺）から転じたのではないかと推測されている。ちなみにこの「渚浜(すはま)」という地名は大東市において知りうる最古の地名である。 == 境内 == [[ファイル:須波麻神社の石灯籠（併用道標）.jpg|サムネイル|[[灯籠|石灯籠]]（併用[[道標]]）]] 社殿手前にはクロガネモチの大木が自生しており、大東市の保存樹木に指定されている。 境内には合計十一基の灯籠があるが、1830年（文政13年）製の境内入り口に置かれた灯籠は道しるべを兼ねたもので、近くの[[東高野街道]]筋から移したものである。 == 現地情報 == ;所在地 *[[大阪府]][[大東市]]中垣内2-11 ;交通アクセス * 最寄駅：JR[[学研都市線]][[野崎駅 (大阪府)|野崎駅]]下車後、徒歩約28分（南東へ約2.2km） * [[近鉄バス]]四條畷線中垣内バス停下車後、徒歩約7分 == 参考文献 == * 古社紀行（摂津・河内・和泉）／國井義典 == 関連項目 == *[[河内国の式内社一覧]] == 外部リンク == * [https://web.archive.org/web/20181001135435/http://www.geocities.jp/engisiki/kawachi/html/040701-01.html　須波麻神社] {{Shinto-stub}} {{神道 横}} [[Category:大阪府の神社]] [[Category:大阪府の旧郷社]] [[Category:河内国の式内社]] [[Category:単立神社]] [[Category:大東市の建築物]] [[Category:大東市の歴史]] ==End Japanese==

[[Category:Shikinai Shōsha]] [[Category:Shrines receiving Hoe and Quiver]] [[Category:Kanpei-sha]] {{translated page|ja|須波麻神社|version=107258089|comment=Imported full ja history}}[[Category:Automerged Japanese text]] == Japanese Wikipedia content == {{神社 |名称=須波麻神社 |画像=[[画像:須波摩神社.jpg|250px]]
境内遠景 |所在地=[[大阪府]][[大東市]]中垣内2-11 |位置= {{WikidataCoord|display=inline,title}} |祭神=[[大国主命]] |社格=[[式内社]]（小）
旧[[郷社]] |創建=不詳 |本殿= |別名= |札所等= |例祭= |神事= |地図 = {{Location map|Japan Osaka|label=須波麻神社|position=bottom|width=200|mark=Shinto torii icon vermillion.svg|marksize=15|float=center|caption=}} {{maplink2|frame=yes|plain=yes|type=point|zoom=12|marker=religious-shinto|frame-align=center|frame-width=200|frame-height=200}} }} '''須波麻神社'''（すはまじんじゃ）は、[[大阪府]][[大東市]]中垣内にある[[神社]]。[[式内社]]で、[[旧社格]]は[[郷社]]。 == 歴史 == [[ファイル:須波麻神社のクロガネモチ.jpg|サムネイル|境内の[[クロガネモチ]]（[[保存樹木]]）]] 言い伝えによると古くは[[出雲大社]]と同体で10月7日に大祭をしていたという。創建の詳細は定かではないが、平安時代の「[[延喜式神名帳]]」には既に記載されており、大東市内で唯一の[[式内社]]である。[[讃良郡]]から[[若江郡]]に至るまで、数十ヶ村の氏子をかかえていた。1873年（明治6年）に讃良郡内十一ヶ村の[[郷社]]になり、1907年（明治40年）9月には寺川の[[大谷神社]]、龍間の[[龍間神社]]、深野の[[坐間神社]]の三社が合祀されたこともあった。現在は中垣内の[[産土神]]である。 ;社名について この神社がある地域は昔[[河内湾]]が存在し、それが[[河内潟]]、[[河内湖]]と[[淀川]]や[[大和川]]などの土砂の流入によって縮小していくことで徐々に集落等がつくられていったが、なおも[[深開池]]や[[深野池]]など大きな池として存在が残った。この神社の西はそうした池の水辺であったとされ、当初は[[大国主命]]ではなく別の水難除けの水神を祀った神社だったという説があり、現在の社名は「渚浜／洲浜(すはま)」（つまり水辺）から転じたのではないかと推測されている。ちなみにこの「渚浜(すはま)」という地名は大東市において知りうる最古の地名である。 == 境内 == [[ファイル:須波麻神社の石灯籠（併用道標）.jpg|サムネイル|[[灯籠|石灯籠]]（併用[[道標]]）]] 社殿手前にはクロガネモチの大木が自生しており、大東市の保存樹木に指定されている。 境内には合計十一基の灯籠があるが、1830年（文政13年）製の境内入り口に置かれた灯籠は道しるべを兼ねたもので、近くの[[東高野街道]]筋から移したものである。 == 現地情報 == ;所在地 *[[大阪府]][[大東市]]中垣内2-11 ;交通アクセス * 最寄駅：JR[[学研都市線]][[野崎駅 (大阪府)|野崎駅]]下車後、徒歩約28分（南東へ約2.2km） * [[近鉄バス]]四條畷線中垣内バス停下車後、徒歩約7分 == 参考文献 == * 古社紀行（摂津・河内・和泉）／國井義典 == 関連項目 == *[[河内国の式内社一覧]] == 外部リンク == * [https://web.archive.org/web/20181001135435/http://www.geocities.jp/engisiki/kawachi/html/040701-01.html　須波麻神社] {{Shinto-stub}} {{神道 横}} ==End Japanese== [[Category:大阪府の神社]] [[Category:大阪府の旧郷社]] [[Category:河内国の式内社]] [[Category:単立神社]] [[Category:大東市の建築物]] [[Category:大東市の歴史]]

旧[[郷社]] |創建=不詳 |本殿= |別名= |札所等= |例祭= |神事= |地図 = {{Location map|Japan Osaka|label=須波麻神社|position=bottom|width=200|mark=Shinto torii icon vermillion.svg|marksize=15|float=center|caption=}} {{maplink2|frame=yes|plain=yes|type=point|zoom=12|marker=religious-shinto|frame-align=center|frame-width=200|frame-height=200}} }} '''須波麻神社'''（すはまじんじゃ）は、[[大阪府]][[大東市]]中垣内にある[[神社]]。[[式内社]]で、[[旧社格]]は[[郷社]]。 == 歴史 == [[ファイル:須波麻神社のクロガネモチ.jpg|サムネイル|境内の[[クロガネモチ]]（[[保存樹木]]）]] 言い伝えによると古くは[[出雲大社]]と同体で10月7日に大祭をしていたという。創建の詳細は定かではないが、平安時代の「[[延喜式神名帳]]」には既に記載されており、大東市内で唯一の[[式内社]]である。[[讃良郡]]から[[若江郡]]に至るまで、数十ヶ村の氏子をかかえていた。1873年（明治6年）に讃良郡内十一ヶ村の[[郷社]]になり、1907年（明治40年）9月には寺川の[[大谷神社]]、龍間の[[龍間神社]]、深野の[[坐間神社]]の三社が合祀されたこともあった。現在は中垣内の[[産土神]]である。 ;社名について この神社がある地域は昔[[河内湾]]が存在し、それが[[河内潟]]、[[河内湖]]と[[淀川]]や[[大和川]]などの土砂の流入によって縮小していくことで徐々に集落等がつくられていったが、なおも[[深開池]]や[[深野池]]など大きな池として存在が残った。この神社の西はそうした池の水辺であったとされ、当初は[[大国主命]]ではなく別の水難除けの水神を祀った神社だったという説があり、現在の社名は「渚浜／洲浜(すはま)」（つまり水辺）から転じたのではないかと推測されている。ちなみにこの「渚浜(すはま)」という地名は大東市において知りうる最古の地名である。 == 境内 == [[ファイル:須波麻神社の石灯籠（併用道標）.jpg|サムネイル|[[灯籠|石灯籠]]（併用[[道標]]）]] 社殿手前にはクロガネモチの大木が自生しており、大東市の保存樹木に指定されている。 境内には合計十一基の灯籠があるが、1830年（文政13年）製の境内入り口に置かれた灯籠は道しるべを兼ねたもので、近くの[[東高野街道]]筋から移したものである。 == 現地情報 == ;所在地 *[[大阪府]][[大東市]]中垣内2-11 ;交通アクセス * 最寄駅：JR[[学研都市線]][[野崎駅 (大阪府)|野崎駅]]下車後、徒歩約28分（南東へ約2.2km） * [[近鉄バス]]四條畷線中垣内バス停下車後、徒歩約7分 == 参考文献 == * 古社紀行（摂津・河内・和泉）／國井義典 == 関連項目 == *[[河内国の式内社一覧]] == 外部リンク == * [https://web.archive.org/web/20181001135435/http://www.geocities.jp/engisiki/kawachi/html/040701-01.html　須波麻神社] {{Shinto-stub}} {{神道 横}} ==End Japanese== [[Category:大阪府の神社]] [[Category:大阪府の旧郷社]] [[Category:河内国の式内社]] [[Category:単立神社]] [[Category:大東市の建築物]] [[Category:大東市の歴史]]

text]] [[Category:Autogenerated pages with jawiki interwikis, possibly accidentally overwritten]] [[Category:Shikinaisha]] [[Category:Shikinai Shōsha]] [[Category:Shrines receiving Hoe and Quiver]] [[Category:Shikinai Hiteisha]] [[Category:Shikinaisha in Kawachi Province]] {{commons category|Suhama-jinja (Daito, Osaka)}} [[JA:須波麻神社]] {{wikidata link|Q11664606}} [[Category:Shikinai Shōsha]] [[Category:Shrines receiving Hoe and Quiver]] [[Category:Kanpei-sha]] {{translated page|ja|須波麻神社|version=107258089|comment=Imported full ja history}}[[Category:Automerged Japanese text]] == Japanese Wikipedia content == {{神社 |名称=須波麻神社 |画像=[[画像:須波摩神社.jpg|250px]]
境内遠景 |所在地={{jalink|大阪府|Q122723}}{{jalink|大東市|Q386668}}中垣内2-11 |位置= {{WikidataCoord|display=inline,title}} |祭神={{jalink|大国主命}} |社格={{jalink|式内社}}（小）
旧{{jalink|郷社}} |創建=不詳 |本殿= |別名= |札所等= |例祭= |神事= |地図 = {{Location map|Japan Osaka|label=須波麻神社|position=bottom|width=200|mark=Shinto torii icon vermillion.svg|marksize=15|float=center|caption=}} {{maplink2|frame=yes|plain=yes|type=point|zoom=12|marker=religious-shinto|frame-align=center|frame-width=200|frame-height=200}} }} '''須波麻神社'''（すはまじんじゃ）は、{{jalink|大阪府|Q122723}}{{jalink|大東市|Q386668}}中垣内にある{{jalink|神社|Q845945}}。{{jalink|式内社}}で、{{jalink|旧社格}}は{{jalink|郷社}}。 == 歴史 == [[ファイル:須波麻神社のクロガネモチ.jpg|サムネイル|境内の{{jalink|クロガネモチ}}（{{jalink|保存樹木}}）]] 言い伝えによると古くは{{jalink|出雲大社|Q696362}}と同体で10月7日に大祭をしていたという。創建の詳細は定かではないが、平安時代の「{{jalink|延喜式神名帳|Q11064932}}」には既に記載されており、大東市内で唯一の{{jalink|式内社}}である。{{jalink|讃良郡|Q11632569}}から{{jalink|若江郡|Q11616589}}に至るまで、数十ヶ村の氏子をかかえていた。1873年（明治6年）に讃良郡内十一ヶ村の{{jalink|郷社}}になり、1907年（明治40年）9月には寺川の{{jalink|大谷神社}}、龍間の{{jalink|龍間神社}}、深野の{{jalink|坐間神社}}の三社が合祀されたこともあった。現在は中垣内の{{jalink|産土神|Q1392097}}である。 ;社名について この神社がある地域は昔{{jalink|河内湾}}が存在し、それが{{jalink|河内潟}}、{{jalink|河内湖|Q11553429}}と{{jalink|淀川|Q1140505}}や{{jalink|大和川|Q9001527}}などの土砂の流入によって縮小していくことで徐々に集落等がつくられていったが、なおも{{jalink|深開池}}や{{jalink|深野池|Q11560330}}など大きな池として存在が残った。この神社の西はそうした池の水辺であったとされ、当初は{{jalink|大国主命}}ではなく別の水難除けの水神を祀った神社だったという説があり、現在の社名は「渚浜／洲浜(すはま)」（つまり水辺）から転じたのではないかと推測されている。ちなみにこの「渚浜(すはま)」という地名は大東市において知りうる最古の地名である。 == 境内 == [[ファイル:須波麻神社の石灯籠（併用道標）.jpg|サムネイル|[[灯籠|石灯籠]]（併用{{jalink|道標|Q22812402}}）]] 社殿手前にはクロガネモチの大木が自生しており、大東市の保存樹木に指定されている。 境内には合計十一基の灯籠があるが、1830年（文政13年）製の境内入り口に置かれた灯籠は道しるべを兼ねたもので、近くの{{jalink|東高野街道|Q11528777}}筋から移したものである。 == 現地情報 == ;所在地 *{{jalink|大阪府|Q122723}}{{jalink|大東市|Q386668}}中垣内2-11 ;交通アクセス * 最寄駅：JR{{jalink|学研都市線}}{{jalink|野崎駅 (大阪府)|Q855914|野崎駅}}下車後、徒歩約28分（南東へ約2.2km） * {{jalink|近鉄バス|Q18636319}}四條畷線中垣内バス停下車後、徒歩約7分 == 参考文献 == * 古社紀行（摂津・河内・和泉）／國井義典 == 関連項目 == *{{jalink|河内国の式内社一覧|Q11553385}} == 外部リンク == * [https://web.archive.org/web/20181001135435/http://www.geocities.jp/engisiki/kawachi/html/040701-01.html　須波麻神社] {{Shinto-stub}} {{神道 横}} ==End Japanese== [[Category:大阪府の神社]] [[JA:須波麻神社]] {{wikidata link|Q11664606}} == Japanese content == {{神社 |名称=須波麻神社 |画像=[[画像:須波摩神社.jpg|250px]]
境内遠景 |所在地={{jalink|大阪府|Q122723}}{{jalink|大東市|Q386668}}中垣内2-11 |位置= {{WikidataCoord|display=inline,title}} |祭神={{jalink|大国主命}} |社格={{jalink|式内社}}（小）
旧{{jalink|郷社|Q134917316}} |創建=不詳 |本殿= |別名= |札所等= |例祭= |神事= |地図 = {{Location map|Japan Osaka|label=須波麻神社|position=bottom|width=200|mark=Shinto torii icon vermillion.svg|marksize=15|float=center|caption=}} {{maplink2|frame=yes|plain=yes|type=point|zoom=12|marker=religious-shinto|frame-align=center|frame-width=200|frame-height=200}} }} '''須波麻神社'''（すはまじんじゃ）は、{{jalink|大阪府|Q122723}}{{jalink|大東市|Q386668}}中垣内にある{{jalink|神社|Q845945}}。{{jalink|式内社}}で、{{jalink|旧社格}}は{{jalink|郷社|Q134917316}}。 === 歴史 === [[ファイル:須波麻神社のクロガネモチ.jpg|サムネイル|境内の{{jalink|クロガネモチ|Q844306}}（{{jalink|保存樹木}}）]] 言い伝えによると古くは{{jalink|出雲大社|Q696362}}と同体で10月7日に大祭をしていたという。創建の詳細は定かではないが、平安時代の「{{jalink|延喜式神名帳|Q11064932}}」には既に記載されており、大東市内で唯一の{{jalink|式内社}}である。{{jalink|讃良郡|Q11632569}}から{{jalink|若江郡|Q11616589}}に至るまで、数十ヶ村の氏子をかかえていた。1873年（明治6年）に讃良郡内十一ヶ村の{{jalink|郷社|Q134917316}}になり、1907年（明治40年）9月には寺川の{{jalink|大谷神社}}、龍間の{{jalink|龍間神社}}、深野の{{jalink|坐間神社}}の三社が合祀されたこともあった。現在は中垣内の{{jalink|産土神|Q1392097}}である。 ;社名について この神社がある地域は昔{{jalink|河内湾}}が存在し、それが{{jalink|河内潟}}、{{jalink|河内湖|Q11553429}}と{{jalink|淀川|Q1140505}}や{{jalink|大和川|Q9001527}}などの土砂の流入によって縮小していくことで徐々に集落等がつくられていったが、なおも{{jalink|深開池}}や{{jalink|深野池|Q11560330}}など大きな池として存在が残った。この神社の西はそうした池の水辺であったとされ、当初は{{jalink|大国主命}}ではなく別の水難除けの水神を祀った神社だったという説があり、現在の社名は「渚浜／洲浜(すはま)」（つまり水辺）から転じたのではないかと推測されている。ちなみにこの「渚浜(すはま)」という地名は大東市において知りうる最古の地名である。 === 境内 === [[ファイル:須波麻神社の石灯籠（併用道標）.jpg|サムネイル|[[灯籠|石灯籠]]（併用{{jalink|道標|Q22812402}}）]] 社殿手前にはクロガネモチの大木が自生しており、大東市の保存樹木に指定されている。 境内には合計十一基の灯籠があるが、1830年（文政13年）製の境内入り口に置かれた灯籠は道しるべを兼ねたもので、近くの{{jalink|東高野街道|Q11528777}}筋から移したものである。 === 現地情報 === ;所在地 *{{jalink|大阪府|Q122723}}{{jalink|大東市|Q386668}}中垣内2-11 ;交通アクセス * 最寄駅：JR{{jalink|学研都市線}}{{jalink|野崎駅 (大阪府)|Q855914|野崎駅}}下車後、徒歩約28分（南東へ約2.2km） * {{jalink|近鉄バス|Q18636319}}四條畷線中垣内バス停下車後、徒歩約7分 === 参考文献 === * 古社紀行（摂津・河内・和泉）／國井義典 === 関連項目 === *{{jalink|河内国の式内社一覧|Q11553385}} === 外部リンク === * [https://web.archive.org/web/20181001135435/http://www.geocities.jp/engisiki/kawachi/html/040701-01.html　須波麻神社] {{Shinto-stub}} {{神道 横}} [[JA:須波麻神社]] {{wikidata link|Q11664606}} == Japanese content == {{Shrine |Name= Suhama Shrine |Image=[[File:Suhama Shrine.jpg|250px]]
Distant view of the shrine grounds |Location={{jalink|Osaka Prefecture|Q122723}}{{jalink|Daitō City|Q386668}} Nakagaito 2-11 |Coordinates= {{WikidataCoord|display=inline,title}} |Enshrined deity={{jalink|Ōkuninushi-no-Mikoto}} |Shrine rank={{jalink|Shikinaisha}} (minor)
Former {{jalink|Gōsha|Q134917316}} |Established=Unknown |Main hall= |Alternative name= |Pilgrimage affiliation= |Annual festival= |Rituals= |Map = {{Location map|Japan Osaka|label=Suhama Shrine|position=bottom|width=200|mark=Shinto torii icon vermillion.svg|marksize=15|float=center|caption=}} {{maplink2|frame=yes|plain=yes|type=point|zoom=12|marker=religious-shinto|frame-align=center|frame-width=200|frame-height=200}} }} '''Suhama Shrine''' (すはまじんじゃ) is a {{jalink|Shinto shrine|Q845945}} located in Nakagaito, {{jalink|Daitō City|Q386668}}, {{jalink|Osaka Prefecture|Q122723}}. It is a {{jalink|Shikinaisha}}, and its former shrine rank was {{jalink|Gōsha|Q134917316}}. === History === [[File:Kurogane-mochi at Suhama Shrine.jpg|Thumbnail|The {{jalink|kurogane-mochi|Q844306}} tree (a {{jalink|protected tree}}) within the shrine grounds]] According to tradition, the shrine was once considered identical in essence to {{jalink|Izumo Taisha|Q696362}} and held its grand festival on October 7. Although the details of its founding are unclear, it is already listed in the Heian-period “{{jalink|Engishiki Jinmyōchō|Q11064932}},” and it is the only {{jalink|Shikinaisha}} within Daitō City. From {{jalink|Sanra District|Q11632569}} to {{jalink|Wakae District|Q11616589}}, it once had parishioners from several dozen villages. In 1873 (Meiji 6), it became the {{jalink|Gōsha|Q134917316}} for eleven villages within Sanra District, and in September 1907 (Meiji 40), three shrines—{{jalink|Ōtani Shrine}} in Teragawa, {{jalink|Ryūma Shrine}} in Ryūma, and {{jalink|Zama Shrine}} in Fukano—were temporarily merged into it. Today, it serves as the {{jalink|ubusuna-gami|Q1392097}} (local tutelary deity) of Nakagaito. ;About the shrine name In ancient times, the area where this shrine stands was part of {{jalink|Kawachi Bay}}, which later became {{jalink|Kawachi Lagoon}}, then {{jalink|Lake Kawachi|Q11553429}}, gradually shrinking due to sediment inflow from rivers such as the {{jalink|Yodo River|Q1140505}} and the {{jalink|Yamato River|Q9001527}}. As this happened, settlements were gradually established, though large ponds such as {{jalink|Fukakai Pond}} and {{jalink|Fukano Pond|Q11560330}} remained. It is said that the western side of this shrine once bordered such waters, and there is a theory that the shrine originally enshrined not {{jalink|Ōkuninushi-no-Mikoto}} but a different water deity to ward off water-related disasters. The current name is thought to derive from “Nagisahama / Suhama” (meaning shoreline or waterside). Incidentally, the place name “Suhama” is the oldest known place name in Daitō City. === Shrine grounds === [[File:Stone lanterns at Suhama Shrine (guidepost).jpg|Thumbnail|[[Lantern|Stone lantern]] (also serving as a {{jalink|guidepost|Q22812402}})]] In front of the shrine buildings stands a large naturally growing kurogane-mochi tree, designated as a protected tree by Daitō City. There are a total of eleven stone lanterns on the grounds. Among them, the lantern placed at the entrance, made in 1830 (Bunsei 13), also served as a guidepost and was relocated from the nearby {{jalink|Higashi Kōya Kaidō|Q11528777}} route. === Local information === ;Address * Nakagaito 2-11, {{jalink|Daitō City|Q386668}}, {{jalink|Osaka Prefecture|Q122723}} ;Access * Nearest station: About a 28-minute walk (approx. 2.2 km southeast) from JR {{jalink|Gakkentoshi Line}} {{jalink|Nozaki Station (Osaka Prefecture)|Q855914|Nozaki Station}} * About a 7-minute walk after getting off at Nakagaito bus stop on the {{jalink|Kintetsu Bus|Q18636319}} Shijōnawate Line === References === * “Kosha Kikō (Settsu, Kawachi, Izumi)” / Yoshinori Kunii === Related items === * {{jalink|List of Shikinaisha in Kawachi Province|Q11553385}} === External links === * [https://web.archive.org/web/20181001135435/http://www.geocities.jp/engisiki/kawachi/html/040701-01.html Suhama Shrine] {{Shinto-stub}} {{Shinto horizontal}} {{DEFAULTSORT:Suhama Shrine}} == Categories == [[Category:Wikidata generated shikinaisha pages]] [[Category:Autogenerated pages with jawiki interwikis, possibly accidentally overwritten]] [[Category:Shinto shrine]] [[Category:Shikinai Shōsha]] [[Category:Shikinai Hiteisha]] [[Category:Shikinaisha in Kawachi Province]] [[Category:pages]] [[Category:大阪府の神社]] [[Category:大阪府の旧郷社]] [[Category:河内国の式内社]] [[Category:単立神社]] [[Category:大東市の建築物]] [[Category:大東市の歴史]]

Distant view of the shrine grounds |Location={{jalink|Osaka Prefecture|Q122723}}{{jalink|Daitō City|Q386668}} Nakagaito 2-11 |Coordinates= {{WikidataCoord|display=inline,title}} |Enshrined deity={{jalink|Ōkuninushi-no-Mikoto}} |Shrine rank={{jalink|Shikinaisha}} (minor)
Former {{jalink|Gōsha|Q134917316}} |Established=Unknown |Main hall= |Alternative name= |Pilgrimage affiliation= |Annual festival= |Rituals= |Map = {{Location map|Japan Osaka|label=Suhama Shrine|position=bottom|width=200|mark=Shinto torii icon vermillion.svg|marksize=15|float=center|caption=}} {{maplink2|frame=yes|plain=yes|type=point|zoom=12|marker=religious-shinto|frame-align=center|frame-width=200|frame-height=200}} }} '''Suhama Shrine''' (すはまじんじゃ) is a {{jalink|Shinto shrine|Q845945}} located in Nakagaito, {{jalink|Daitō City|Q386668}}, {{jalink|Osaka Prefecture|Q122723}}. It is a {{jalink|Shikinaisha}}, and its former shrine rank was {{jalink|Gōsha|Q134917316}}. === History === [[File:Kurogane-mochi at Suhama Shrine.jpg|Thumbnail|The {{jalink|kurogane-mochi|Q844306}} tree (a {{jalink|protected tree}}) within the shrine grounds]] According to tradition, the shrine was once considered identical in essence to {{jalink|Izumo Taisha|Q696362}} and held its grand festival on October 7. Although the details of its founding are unclear, it is already listed in the Heian-period “{{jalink|Engishiki Jinmyōchō|Q11064932}},” and it is the only {{jalink|Shikinaisha}} within Daitō City. From {{jalink|Sanra District|Q11632569}} to {{jalink|Wakae District|Q11616589}}, it once had parishioners from several dozen villages. In 1873 (Meiji 6), it became the {{jalink|Gōsha|Q134917316}} for eleven villages within Sanra District, and in September 1907 (Meiji 40), three shrines—{{jalink|Ōtani Shrine}} in Teragawa, {{jalink|Ryūma Shrine}} in Ryūma, and {{jalink|Zama Shrine}} in Fukano—were temporarily merged into it. Today, it serves as the {{jalink|ubusuna-gami|Q1392097}} (local tutelary deity) of Nakagaito. ;About the shrine name In ancient times, the area where this shrine stands was part of {{jalink|Kawachi Bay}}, which later became {{jalink|Kawachi Lagoon}}, then {{jalink|Lake Kawachi|Q11553429}}, gradually shrinking due to sediment inflow from rivers such as the {{jalink|Yodo River|Q1140505}} and the {{jalink|Yamato River|Q9001527}}. As this happened, settlements were gradually established, though large ponds such as {{jalink|Fukakai Pond}} and {{jalink|Fukano Pond|Q11560330}} remained. It is said that the western side of this shrine once bordered such waters, and there is a theory that the shrine originally enshrined not {{jalink|Ōkuninushi-no-Mikoto}} but a different water deity to ward off water-related disasters. The current name is thought to derive from “Nagisahama / Suhama” (meaning shoreline or waterside). Incidentally, the place name “Suhama” is the oldest known place name in Daitō City. === Shrine grounds === [[File:Stone lanterns at Suhama Shrine (guidepost).jpg|Thumbnail|[[Lantern|Stone lantern]] (also serving as a {{jalink|guidepost|Q22812402}})]] In front of the shrine buildings stands a large naturally growing kurogane-mochi tree, designated as a protected tree by Daitō City. There are a total of eleven stone lanterns on the grounds. Among them, the lantern placed at the entrance, made in 1830 (Bunsei 13), also served as a guidepost and was relocated from the nearby {{jalink|Higashi Kōya Kaidō|Q11528777}} route. === Local information === ;Address * Nakagaito 2-11, {{jalink|Daitō City|Q386668}}, {{jalink|Osaka Prefecture|Q122723}} ;Access * Nearest station: About a 28-minute walk (approx. 2.2 km southeast) from JR {{jalink|Gakkentoshi Line}} {{jalink|Nozaki Station (Osaka Prefecture)|Q855914|Nozaki Station}} * About a 7-minute walk after getting off at Nakagaito bus stop on the {{jalink|Kintetsu Bus|Q18636319}} Shijōnawate Line === References === * “Kosha Kikō (Settsu, Kawachi, Izumi)” / Yoshinori Kunii === Related items === * {{jalink|List of Shikinaisha in Kawachi Province|Q11553385}} === External links === * [https://web.archive.org/web/20181001135435/http://www.geocities.jp/engisiki/kawachi/html/040701-01.html Suhama Shrine] {{Shinto-stub}} {{Shinto horizontal}} {{DEFAULTSORT:Suhama Shrine}} == Categories == [[Category:Wikidata generated shikinaisha pages]] [[Category:Autogenerated pages with jawiki interwikis, possibly accidentally overwritten]] [[Category:Shinto shrine]] [[Category:Shikinai Shōsha]] [[Category:Shikinai Hiteisha]] [[Category:Shikinaisha in Kawachi Province]] [[Category:pages]] [[Category:大阪府の神社]] [[Category:大阪府の旧郷社]] [[Category:河内国の式内社]] [[Category:単立神社]] [[Category:大東市の建築物]] [[Category:大東市の歴史]]

As this happened, settlements were gradually established, though large ponds such as {{jalink|Fukakai Pond}} and {{jalink|Fukano Pond|Q11560330}} remained. It is said that the western side of this shrine once bordered such waters, and there is a theory that the shrine originally enshrined not {{jalink|Ōkuninushi-no-Mikoto}} but a different water deity to ward off water-related disasters. The current name is thought to derive from “Nagisahama / Suhama” (meaning shoreline or waterside). Incidentally, the place name “Suhama” is the oldest known place name in Daitō City. === Shrine grounds === [[File:Stone lanterns at Suhama Shrine (guidepost).jpg|Thumbnail|[[Lantern|Stone lantern]] (also serving as a {{jalink|guidepost|Q22812402}})]] In front of the shrine buildings stands a large naturally growing kurogane-mochi tree, designated as a protected tree by Daitō City. There are a total of eleven stone lanterns on the grounds. Among them, the lantern placed at the entrance, made in 1830 (Bunsei 13), also served as a guidepost and was relocated from the nearby {{jalink|Higashi Kōya Kaidō|Q11528777}} route. === Local information === ;Address * Nakagaito 2-11, {{jalink|Daitō City|Q386668}}, {{jalink|Osaka Prefecture|Q122723}} ;Access * Nearest station: About a 28-minute walk (approx. 2.2 km southeast) from JR {{jalink|Gakkentoshi Line}} {{jalink|Nozaki Station (Osaka Prefecture)|Q855914|Nozaki Station}} * About a 7-minute walk after getting off at Nakagaito bus stop on the {{jalink|Kintetsu Bus|Q18636319}} Shijōnawate Line === References === * “Kosha Kikō (Settsu, Kawachi, Izumi)” / Yoshinori Kunii === Related items === * {{jalink|List of Shikinaisha in Kawachi Province|Q11553385}} === External links === * [https://web.archive.org/web/20181001135435/http://www.geocities.jp/engisiki/kawachi/html/040701-01.html Suhama Shrine] {{Shinto-stub}} {{Shinto horizontal}} {{DEFAULTSORT:Suhama Shrine}} == Categories == [[Category:Wikidata generated shikinaisha pages|Suhama Shrine (Daito, Osaka)]][[Category:Shikinaisha]] [[Category:Shinto shrine]] [[Category:Shikinai Shōsha]] [[Category:Shikinai Hiteisha]] [[Category:Shikinaisha in Kawachi Province]] [[Category:pages]] [[Category:大阪府の神社]] [[Category:大阪府の旧郷社]] [[Category:河内国の式内社]] [[Category:単立神社]] [[Category:大東市の建築物]] [[Category:大東市の歴史]]

It is a {{jalink|Shikinaisha}}, and its former shrine rank was {{jalink|Gōsha|Q134917316}}. === History === [[File:Kurogane-mochi at Suhama Shrine.jpg|Thumbnail|The {{jalink|kurogane-mochi|Q844306}} tree (a {{jalink|protected tree}}) within the shrine grounds]] According to tradition, the shrine was once considered identical in essence to {{jalink|Izumo Taisha|Q696362}} and held its grand festival on October 7. Although the details of its founding are unclear, it is already listed in the Heian-period “{{jalink|Engishiki Jinmyōchō|Q11064932}},” and it is the only {{jalink|Shikinaisha}} within Daitō City. From {{jalink|Sanra District|Q11632569}} to {{jalink|Wakae District|Q11616589}}, it once had parishioners from several dozen villages. In 1873 (Meiji 6), it became the {{jalink|Gōsha|Q134917316}} for eleven villages within Sanra District, and in September 1907 (Meiji 40), three shrines—{{jalink|Ōtani Shrine}} in Teragawa, {{jalink|Ryūma Shrine}} in Ryūma, and {{jalink|Zama Shrine}} in Fukano—were temporarily merged into it. Today, it serves as the {{jalink|ubusuna-gami|Q1392097}} (local tutelary deity) of Nakagaito. ;About the shrine name In ancient times, the area where this shrine stands was part of {{jalink|Kawachi Bay}}, which later became {{jalink|Kawachi Lagoon}}, then {{jalink|Lake Kawachi|Q11553429}}, gradually shrinking due to sediment inflow from rivers such as the {{jalink|Yodo River|Q1140505}} and the {{jalink|Yamato River|Q9001527}}. As this happened, settlements were gradually established, though large ponds such as {{jalink|Fukakai Pond}} and {{jalink|Fukano Pond|Q11560330}} remained. It is said that the western side of this shrine once bordered such waters, and there is a theory that the shrine originally enshrined not {{jalink|Ōkuninushi-no-Mikoto}} but a different water deity to ward off water-related disasters. The current name is thought to derive from “Nagisahama / Suhama” (meaning shoreline or waterside). Incidentally, the place name “Suhama” is the oldest known place name in Daitō City. === Shrine grounds === [[File:Stone lanterns at Suhama Shrine (guidepost).jpg|Thumbnail|[[Lantern|Stone lantern]] (also serving as a {{jalink|guidepost|Q22812402}})]] In front of the shrine buildings stands a large naturally growing kurogane-mochi tree, designated as a protected tree by Daitō City. There are a total of eleven stone lanterns on the grounds. Among them, the lantern placed at the entrance, made in 1830 (Bunsei 13), also served as a guidepost and was relocated from the nearby {{jalink|Higashi Kōya Kaidō|Q11528777}} route. === Local information === ;Address * Nakagaito 2-11, {{jalink|Daitō City|Q386668}}, {{jalink|Osaka Prefecture|Q122723}} ;Access * Nearest station: About a 28-minute walk (approx. 2.2 km southeast) from JR {{jalink|Gakkentoshi Line}} {{jalink|Nozaki Station (Osaka Prefecture)|Q855914|Nozaki Station}} * About a 7-minute walk after getting off at Nakagaito bus stop on the {{jalink|Kintetsu Bus|Q18636319}} Shijōnawate Line === References === * “Kosha Kikō (Settsu, Kawachi, Izumi)” / Yoshinori Kunii === Related items === * {{jalink|List of Shikinaisha in Kawachi Province|Q11553385}} === External links === * [https://web.archive.org/web/20181001135435/http://www.geocities.jp/engisiki/kawachi/html/040701-01.html Suhama Shrine] {{Shinto-stub}} {{Shinto horizontal}} {{DEFAULTSORT:Suhama Shrine}} == Categories == [[Category:Wikidata generated shikinaisha pages|Suhama Shrine (Daito, Osaka)]][[Category:Shikinaisha|Suhama Shrine (Daito, Osaka)]][[Category:Pages]] [[Category:Shinto shrine]] [[Category:Shikinai Shōsha]] [[Category:Shikinai Hiteisha]] [[Category:Shikinaisha in Kawachi Province]] [[Category:pages]] [[Category:大阪府の神社]] [[Category:大阪府の旧郷社]] [[Category:河内国の式内社]] [[Category:単立神社]] [[Category:大東市の建築物]] [[Category:大東市の歴史]]

Among them, the lantern placed at the entrance, made in 1830 (Bunsei 13), also served as a guidepost and was relocated from the nearby {{jalink|Higashi Kōya Kaidō|Q11528777}} route. === Local information === ;Address * Nakagaito 2-11, {{jalink|Daitō City|Q386668}}, {{jalink|Osaka Prefecture|Q122723}} ;Access * Nearest station: About a 28-minute walk (approx. 2.2 km southeast) from JR {{jalink|Gakkentoshi Line}} {{jalink|Nozaki Station (Osaka Prefecture)|Q855914|Nozaki Station}} * About a 7-minute walk after getting off at Nakagaito bus stop on the {{jalink|Kintetsu Bus|Q18636319}} Shijōnawate Line === References === * “Kosha Kikō (Settsu, Kawachi, Izumi)” / Yoshinori Kunii === Related items === * {{jalink|List of Shikinaisha in Kawachi Province|Q11553385}} === External links === * [https://web.archive.org/web/20181001135435/http://www.geocities.jp/engisiki/kawachi/html/040701-01.html Suhama Shrine] {{Shinto-stub}} {{Shinto horizontal}} {{DEFAULTSORT:Suhama Shrine}} == Categories == [[Category:Wikidata generated shikinaisha pages|Suhama Shrine (Daito, Osaka)]][[Category:Shikinaisha|Suhama Shrine (Daito, Osaka)]][[Category:pages]][[Category:pages]] [[Category:Shinto shrine]] [[Category:Shikinai Shōsha]] [[Category:Shikinai Hiteisha]] [[Category:Shikinaisha in Kawachi Province]] [[Category:pages]] [[Category:大阪府の神社]] [[Category:大阪府の旧郷社]] [[Category:河内国の式内社]] [[Category:単立神社]] [[Category:大東市の建築物]] [[Category:大東市の歴史]]

[[JA:須波麻神社]] {{wikidata link|Q11664606}} == Japanese content == {{Shrine |Name= Suhama Shrine |Image=[[File:Suhama Shrine.jpg|250px]]
Distant view of the shrine grounds |Location={{ill|lt=Osaka Prefecture|Q122723|WD=Q122723}}{{ill|lt=Daitō City|Q386668|WD=Q386668}} Nakagaito 2-11 |Coordinates= {{WikidataCoord|display=inline,title}} |Enshrined deity={{ill||lt=Ōkuninushi-no-Mikoto|UNKNOWN}} |Shrine rank={{ill||lt=Shikinaisha|UNKNOWN}} (minor)
Former {{ill|lt=Gōsha|Q134917316|WD=Q134917316}} |Established=Unknown |Main hall= |Alternative name= |Pilgrimage affiliation= |Annual festival= |Rituals= |Map = {{Location map|Japan Osaka|label=Suhama Shrine|position=bottom|width=200|mark=Shinto torii icon vermillion.svg|marksize=15|float=center|caption=}} {{maplink2|frame=yes|plain=yes|type=point|zoom=12|marker=religious-shinto|frame-align=center|frame-width=200|frame-height=200}} }} '''Suhama Shrine''' (すはまじんじゃ) is a {{ill|lt=Shinto shrine|Q845945|WD=Q845945}} located in Nakagaito, {{ill|lt=Daitō City|Q386668|WD=Q386668}}, {{ill|lt=Osaka Prefecture|Q122723|WD=Q122723}}. It is a {{ill||lt=Shikinaisha|UNKNOWN}}, and its former shrine rank was {{ill|lt=Gōsha|Q134917316|WD=Q134917316}}. === History === [[File:Kurogane-mochi at Suhama Shrine.jpg|Thumbnail|The {{ill|lt=kurogane-mochi|Q844306|WD=Q844306}} tree (a {{ill||lt=protected tree|UNKNOWN}}) within the shrine grounds]] According to tradition, the shrine was once considered identical in essence to {{ill|lt=Izumo Taisha|Q696362|WD=Q696362}} and held its grand festival on October 7. Although the details of its founding are unclear, it is already listed in the Heian-period “{{ill|lt=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|Q11064932|WD=Q11064932}},” and it is the only {{ill||lt=Shikinaisha|UNKNOWN}} within Daitō City. From {{ill|lt=Sanra District|Q11632569|WD=Q11632569}} to {{ill|lt=Wakae District|Q11616589|WD=Q11616589}}, it once had parishioners from several dozen villages. In 1873 (Meiji 6), it became the {{ill|lt=Gōsha|Q134917316|WD=Q134917316}} for eleven villages within Sanra District, and in September 1907 (Meiji 40), three shrines—{{ill||lt=Ōtani Shrine|UNKNOWN}} in Teragawa, {{ill||lt=Ryūma Shrine|UNKNOWN}} in Ryūma, and {{ill||lt=Zama Shrine|UNKNOWN}} in Fukano—were temporarily merged into it. Today, it serves as the {{ill|lt=ubusuna-gami|Q1392097|WD=Q1392097}} (local tutelary deity) of Nakagaito. ;About the shrine name In ancient times, the area where this shrine stands was part of {{ill||lt=Kawachi Bay|UNKNOWN}}, which later became {{ill||lt=Kawachi Lagoon|UNKNOWN}}, then {{ill|lt=Lake Kawachi|Q11553429|WD=Q11553429}}, gradually shrinking due to sediment inflow from rivers such as the {{ill|lt=Yodo River|Q1140505|WD=Q1140505}} and the {{ill|lt=Yamato River|Q9001527|WD=Q9001527}}. As this happened, settlements were gradually established, though large ponds such as {{ill||lt=Fukakai Pond|UNKNOWN}} and {{ill|lt=Fukano Pond|Q11560330|WD=Q11560330}} remained. It is said that the western side of this shrine once bordered such waters, and there is a theory that the shrine originally enshrined not {{ill||lt=Ōkuninushi-no-Mikoto|UNKNOWN}} but a different water deity to ward off water-related disasters. The current name is thought to derive from “Nagisahama / Suhama” (meaning shoreline or waterside). Incidentally, the place name “Suhama” is the oldest known place name in Daitō City. === Shrine grounds === [[File:Stone lanterns at Suhama Shrine (guidepost).jpg|Thumbnail|[[Lantern|Stone lantern]] (also serving as a {{ill|lt=guidepost|Q22812402|WD=Q22812402}})]] In front of the shrine buildings stands a large naturally growing kurogane-mochi tree, designated as a protected tree by Daitō City. There are a total of eleven stone lanterns on the grounds. Among them, the lantern placed at the entrance, made in 1830 (Bunsei 13), also served as a guidepost and was relocated from the nearby {{ill|lt=Higashi Kōya Kaidō|Q11528777|WD=Q11528777}} route. === Local information === ;Address * Nakagaito 2-11, {{ill|lt=Daitō City|Q386668|WD=Q386668}}, {{ill|lt=Osaka Prefecture|Q122723|WD=Q122723}} ;Access * Nearest station: About a 28-minute walk (approx. 2.2 km southeast) from JR {{ill||lt=Gakkentoshi Line|UNKNOWN}} {{ill|lt=Nozaki Station (Osaka Prefecture)|Q855914|WD=Q855914|lt=Nozaki Station}} * About a 7-minute walk after getting off at Nakagaito bus stop on the {{ill|lt=Kintetsu Bus|Q18636319|WD=Q18636319}} Shijōnawate Line === References === * “Kosha Kikō (Settsu, Kawachi, Izumi)” / Yoshinori Kunii === Related items === * {{ill|lt=List of Shikinaisha in Kawachi Province|Q11553385|WD=Q11553385}} === External links === * [https://web.archive.org/web/20181001135435/http://www.geocities.jp/engisiki/kawachi/html/040701-01.html Suhama Shrine] {{Shinto-stub}} {{Shinto horizontal}} {{DEFAULTSORT:Suhama Shrine}} == Categories == [[Category:Wikidata generated shikinaisha pages|Suhama Shrine (Daito, Osaka)]][[Category:Shikinaisha|Suhama Shrine (Daito, Osaka)]][[Category:pages]][[Category:pages]] [[Category:Shinto shrine]] [[Category:Shikinai Shōsha]] [[Category:Shikinai Hiteisha]] [[Category:Shikinaisha in Kawachi Province]] [[Category:pages]] [[Category:大阪府の神社]] [[Category:大阪府の旧郷社]] [[Category:河内国の式内社]] [[Category:単立神社]] [[Category:大東市の建築物]] [[Category:大東市の歴史]]

Among them, the lantern placed at the entrance, made in 1830 (Bunsei 13), also served as a guidepost and was relocated from the nearby {{ill|lt=Higashi Kōya Kaidō|Q11528777|WD=Q11528777}} route. === Local information === ;Address * Nakagaito 2-11, {{ill|lt=Daitō City|Q386668|WD=Q386668}}, {{ill|lt=Osaka Prefecture|Q122723|WD=Q122723}} ;Access * Nearest station: About a 28-minute walk (approx. 2.2 km southeast) from JR {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Gakkentoshi Line|UNKNOWN}} {{ill|lt=Nozaki Station (Osaka Prefecture)|Q855914|WD=Q855914|lt=Nozaki Station}} * About a 7-minute walk after getting off at Nakagaito bus stop on the {{ill|lt=Kintetsu Bus|Q18636319|WD=Q18636319}} Shijōnawate Line === References === * “Kosha Kikō (Settsu, Kawachi, Izumi)” / Yoshinori Kunii === Related items === * {{ill|lt=List of Shikinaisha in Kawachi Province|Q11553385|WD=Q11553385}} === External links === * [https://web.archive.org/web/20181001135435/http://www.geocities.jp/engisiki/kawachi/html/040701-01.html Suhama Shrine] {{Shinto-stub}} {{Shinto horizontal}} {{DEFAULTSORT:Suhama Shrine}} == Categories == [[Category:Wikidata generated shikinaisha pages|Suhama Shrine (Daito, Osaka)]][[Category:Shikinaisha|Suhama Shrine (Daito, Osaka)]][[Category:pages]][[Category:pages]] [[Category:Shinto shrine]] [[Category:Shikinai Shōsha]] [[Category:Shikinai Hiteisha]] [[Category:Shikinaisha in Kawachi Province]] [[Category:pages]] [[Category:大阪府の神社]] [[Category:大阪府の旧郷社]] [[Category:河内国の式内社]] [[Category:単立神社]] [[Category:大東市の建築物]] [[Category:大東市の歴史]]

[[JA:須波麻神社]] {{wikidata link|Q11664606}} == Japanese content == {{Shrine |Name= Suhama Shrine |Image=[[File:Suhama Shrine.jpg|250px]]
Distant view of the shrine grounds |Location={{ill|lt=Osaka Prefecture|Q122723|WD=Q122723}}{{ill|lt=Daitō City|Q386668|WD=Q386668}} Nakagaito 2-11 |Coordinates= {{WikidataCoord|display=inline,title}} |Enshrined deity={{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Ōkuninushi-no-Mikoto|1=UNKNOWN}} |Shrine rank={{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Shikinaisha|1=UNKNOWN}} (minor)
Former {{ill|lt=Gōsha|Q134917316|WD=Q134917316}} |Established=Unknown |Main hall= |Alternative name= |Pilgrimage affiliation= |Annual festival= |Rituals= |Map = {{Location map|Japan Osaka|label=Suhama Shrine|position=bottom|width=200|mark=Shinto torii icon vermillion.svg|marksize=15|float=center|caption=}} {{maplink2|frame=yes|plain=yes|type=point|zoom=12|marker=religious-shinto|frame-align=center|frame-width=200|frame-height=200}} }} '''Suhama Shrine''' (すはまじんじゃ) is a {{ill|lt=Shinto shrine|Q845945|WD=Q845945}} located in Nakagaito, {{ill|lt=Daitō City|Q386668|WD=Q386668}}, {{ill|lt=Osaka Prefecture|Q122723|WD=Q122723}}. It is a {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Shikinaisha|1=UNKNOWN}}, and its former shrine rank was {{ill|lt=Gōsha|Q134917316|WD=Q134917316}}. === History === [[File:Kurogane-mochi at Suhama Shrine.jpg|Thumbnail|The {{ill|lt=kurogane-mochi|Q844306|WD=Q844306}} tree (a {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=protected tree|1=UNKNOWN}}) within the shrine grounds]] According to tradition, the shrine was once considered identical in essence to {{ill|lt=Izumo Taisha|Q696362|WD=Q696362}} and held its grand festival on October 7. Although the details of its founding are unclear, it is already listed in the Heian-period “{{ill|lt=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|Q11064932|WD=Q11064932}},” and it is the only {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Shikinaisha|1=UNKNOWN}} within Daitō City. From {{ill|lt=Sanra District|Q11632569|WD=Q11632569}} to {{ill|lt=Wakae District|Q11616589|WD=Q11616589}}, it once had parishioners from several dozen villages. In 1873 (Meiji 6), it became the {{ill|lt=Gōsha|Q134917316|WD=Q134917316}} for eleven villages within Sanra District, and in September 1907 (Meiji 40), three shrines—{{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Ōtani Shrine|1=UNKNOWN}} in Teragawa, {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Ryūma Shrine|1=UNKNOWN}} in Ryūma, and {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Zama Shrine|1=UNKNOWN}} in Fukano—were temporarily merged into it. Today, it serves as the {{ill|lt=ubusuna-gami|Q1392097|WD=Q1392097}} (local tutelary deity) of Nakagaito. ;About the shrine name In ancient times, the area where this shrine stands was part of {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Kawachi Bay|1=UNKNOWN}}, which later became {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Kawachi Lagoon|1=UNKNOWN}}, then {{ill|lt=Lake Kawachi|Q11553429|WD=Q11553429}}, gradually shrinking due to sediment inflow from rivers such as the {{ill|lt=Yodo River|Q1140505|WD=Q1140505}} and the {{ill|lt=Yamato River|Q9001527|WD=Q9001527}}. As this happened, settlements were gradually established, though large ponds such as {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Fukakai Pond|1=UNKNOWN}} and {{ill|lt=Fukano Pond|Q11560330|WD=Q11560330}} remained. It is said that the western side of this shrine once bordered such waters, and there is a theory that the shrine originally enshrined not {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Ōkuninushi-no-Mikoto|1=UNKNOWN}} but a different water deity to ward off water-related disasters. The current name is thought to derive from “Nagisahama / Suhama” (meaning shoreline or waterside). Incidentally, the place name “Suhama” is the oldest known place name in Daitō City. === Shrine grounds === [[File:Stone lanterns at Suhama Shrine (guidepost).jpg|Thumbnail|[[Lantern|Stone lantern]] (also serving as a {{ill|lt=guidepost|Q22812402|WD=Q22812402}})]] In front of the shrine buildings stands a large naturally growing kurogane-mochi tree, designated as a protected tree by Daitō City. There are a total of eleven stone lanterns on the grounds. Among them, the lantern placed at the entrance, made in 1830 (Bunsei 13), also served as a guidepost and was relocated from the nearby {{ill|lt=Higashi Kōya Kaidō|Q11528777|WD=Q11528777}} route. === Local information === ;Address * Nakagaito 2-11, {{ill|lt=Daitō City|Q386668|WD=Q386668}}, {{ill|lt=Osaka Prefecture|Q122723|WD=Q122723}} ;Access * Nearest station: About a 28-minute walk (approx. 2.2 km southeast) from JR {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Gakkentoshi Line|1=UNKNOWN}} {{ill|lt=Nozaki Station (Osaka Prefecture)|Q855914|WD=Q855914|lt=Nozaki Station}} * About a 7-minute walk after getting off at Nakagaito bus stop on the {{ill|lt=Kintetsu Bus|Q18636319|WD=Q18636319}} Shijōnawate Line === References === * “Kosha Kikō (Settsu, Kawachi, Izumi)” / Yoshinori Kunii === Related items === * {{ill|lt=List of Shikinaisha in Kawachi Province|Q11553385|WD=Q11553385}} === External links === * [https://web.archive.org/web/20181001135435/http://www.geocities.jp/engisiki/kawachi/html/040701-01.html Suhama Shrine] {{Shinto-stub}} {{Shinto horizontal}} {{DEFAULTSORT:Suhama Shrine}} == Categories == [[Category:Wikidata generated shikinaisha pages|Suhama Shrine (Daito, Osaka)]][[Category:Shikinaisha|Suhama Shrine (Daito, Osaka)]][[Category:pages]][[Category:pages]] [[Category:Shinto shrine]] [[Category:Shikinai Shōsha]] [[Category:Shikinai Hiteisha]] [[Category:Shikinaisha in Kawachi Province]] [[Category:pages]] [[Category:大阪府の神社]] [[Category:大阪府の旧郷社]] [[Category:河内国の式内社]] [[Category:単立神社]] [[Category:大東市の建築物]] [[Category:大東市の歴史]]

Among them, the lantern placed at the entrance, made in 1830 (Bunsei 13), also served as a guidepost and was relocated from the nearby {{ill|lt=Higashi Kōya Kaidō|Q11528777|WD=Q11528777}} route. === Local information === ;Address * Nakagaito 2-11, {{ill|lt=Daitō City|Q386668|WD=Q386668}}, {{ill|lt=Osaka Prefecture|Q122723|WD=Q122723}} ;Access * Nearest station: About a 28-minute walk (approx. 2.2 km southeast) from JR {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Gakkentoshi Line|1=UNKNOWN}} {{ill|lt=Nozaki Station (Osaka Prefecture)|Q855914|WD=Q855914|lt=Nozaki Station}} * About a 7-minute walk after getting off at Nakagaito bus stop on the {{ill|lt=Kintetsu Bus|Q18636319|WD=Q18636319}} Shijōnawate Line === References === * “Kosha Kikō (Settsu, Kawachi, Izumi)” / Yoshinori Kunii === Related items === * {{ill|lt=List of Shikinaisha in Kawachi Province|Q11553385|WD=Q11553385}} === External links === * [https://web.archive.org/web/20181001135435/http://www.geocities.jp/engisiki/kawachi/html/040701-01.html Suhama Shrine] {{Shinto-stub}} {{Shinto horizontal}} {{DEFAULTSORT:Suhama Shrine}} == Categories == [[Category:Wikidata generated shikinaisha pages|Suhama Shrine (Daito, Osaka)]][[Category:Shikinaisha|Suhama Shrine (Daito, Osaka)]][[Category:pages]][[Category:pages]] [[Category:Shinto shrine]] [[Category:Shikinai Shōsha]] [[Category:Shikinai Hiteisha]] [[Category:Shikinaisha in Kawachi Province]] [[Category:pages]] [[Category:大阪府の神社]] [[Category:大阪府の旧郷社]] [[Category:河内国の式内社]] [[Category:単立神社]] [[Category:大東市の建築物]] [[Category:大東市の歴史]]

[[JA:須波麻神社]] {{wikidata link|Q11664606}} == Japanese content == {{Shrine |Name= Suhama Shrine |Image=[[File:Suhama Shrine.jpg|250px]]
Distant view of the shrine grounds |Location={{ill|lt=Osaka Prefecture|Q122723|WD=Q122723}}{{ill|lt=Daitō City|Q386668|WD=Q386668}} Nakagaito 2-11 |Coordinates= {{WikidataCoord|display=inline,title}} |Enshrined deity={{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Ōkuninushi-no-Mikoto|1=UNKNOWN|WD=Q276944}} |Shrine rank={{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Shikinaisha|1=UNKNOWN|WD=Q134917286}} (minor)
Former {{ill|lt=Gōsha|Q134917316|WD=Q134917316}} |Established=Unknown |Main hall= |Alternative name= |Pilgrimage affiliation= |Annual festival= |Rituals= |Map = {{Location map|Japan Osaka|label=Suhama Shrine|position=bottom|width=200|mark=Shinto torii icon vermillion.svg|marksize=15|float=center|caption=}} {{maplink2|frame=yes|plain=yes|type=point|zoom=12|marker=religious-shinto|frame-align=center|frame-width=200|frame-height=200}} }} '''Suhama Shrine''' (すはまじんじゃ) is a {{ill|lt=Shinto shrine|Q845945|WD=Q845945}} located in Nakagaito, {{ill|lt=Daitō City|Q386668|WD=Q386668}}, {{ill|lt=Osaka Prefecture|Q122723|WD=Q122723}}. It is a {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Shikinaisha|1=UNKNOWN|WD=Q134917286}}, and its former shrine rank was {{ill|lt=Gōsha|Q134917316|WD=Q134917316}}. === History === [[File:Kurogane-mochi at Suhama Shrine.jpg|Thumbnail|The {{ill|lt=kurogane-mochi|Q844306|WD=Q844306}} tree (a {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=protected tree|1=UNKNOWN}}) within the shrine grounds]] According to tradition, the shrine was once considered identical in essence to {{ill|lt=Izumo Taisha|Q696362|WD=Q696362}} and held its grand festival on October 7. Although the details of its founding are unclear, it is already listed in the Heian-period “{{ill|lt=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|Q11064932|WD=Q11064932}},” and it is the only {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Shikinaisha|1=UNKNOWN|WD=Q134917286}} within Daitō City. From {{ill|lt=Sanra District|Q11632569|WD=Q11632569}} to {{ill|lt=Wakae District|Q11616589|WD=Q11616589}}, it once had parishioners from several dozen villages. In 1873 (Meiji 6), it became the {{ill|lt=Gōsha|Q134917316|WD=Q134917316}} for eleven villages within Sanra District, and in September 1907 (Meiji 40), three shrines—{{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Ōtani Shrine|1=UNKNOWN}} in Teragawa, {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Ryūma Shrine|1=UNKNOWN}} in Ryūma, and {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Zama Shrine|1=UNKNOWN}} in Fukano—were temporarily merged into it. Today, it serves as the {{ill|lt=ubusuna-gami|Q1392097|WD=Q1392097}} (local tutelary deity) of Nakagaito. ;About the shrine name In ancient times, the area where this shrine stands was part of {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Kawachi Bay|1=UNKNOWN}}, which later became {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Kawachi Lagoon|1=UNKNOWN}}, then {{ill|lt=Lake Kawachi|Q11553429|WD=Q11553429}}, gradually shrinking due to sediment inflow from rivers such as the {{ill|lt=Yodo River|Q1140505|WD=Q1140505}} and the {{ill|lt=Yamato River|Q9001527|WD=Q9001527}}. As this happened, settlements were gradually established, though large ponds such as {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Fukakai Pond|1=UNKNOWN}} and {{ill|lt=Fukano Pond|Q11560330|WD=Q11560330}} remained. It is said that the western side of this shrine once bordered such waters, and there is a theory that the shrine originally enshrined not {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Ōkuninushi-no-Mikoto|1=UNKNOWN|WD=Q276944}} but a different water deity to ward off water-related disasters. The current name is thought to derive from “Nagisahama / Suhama” (meaning shoreline or waterside). Incidentally, the place name “Suhama” is the oldest known place name in Daitō City. === Shrine grounds === [[File:Stone lanterns at Suhama Shrine (guidepost).jpg|Thumbnail|[[Lantern|Stone lantern]] (also serving as a {{ill|lt=guidepost|Q22812402|WD=Q22812402}})]] In front of the shrine buildings stands a large naturally growing kurogane-mochi tree, designated as a protected tree by Daitō City. There are a total of eleven stone lanterns on the grounds. Among them, the lantern placed at the entrance, made in 1830 (Bunsei 13), also served as a guidepost and was relocated from the nearby {{ill|lt=Higashi Kōya Kaidō|Q11528777|WD=Q11528777}} route. === Local information === ;Address * Nakagaito 2-11, {{ill|lt=Daitō City|Q386668|WD=Q386668}}, {{ill|lt=Osaka Prefecture|Q122723|WD=Q122723}} ;Access * Nearest station: About a 28-minute walk (approx. 2.2 km southeast) from JR {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Gakkentoshi Line|1=UNKNOWN}} {{ill|lt=Nozaki Station (Osaka Prefecture)|Q855914|WD=Q855914|lt=Nozaki Station}} * About a 7-minute walk after getting off at Nakagaito bus stop on the {{ill|lt=Kintetsu Bus|Q18636319|WD=Q18636319}} Shijōnawate Line === References === * “Kosha Kikō (Settsu, Kawachi, Izumi)” / Yoshinori Kunii === Related items === * {{ill|lt=List of Shikinaisha in Kawachi Province|Q11553385|WD=Q11553385}} === External links === * [https://web.archive.org/web/20181001135435/http://www.geocities.jp/engisiki/kawachi/html/040701-01.html Suhama Shrine] {{Shinto-stub}} {{Shinto horizontal}} {{DEFAULTSORT:Suhama Shrine}} == Categories == [[Category:Wikidata generated shikinaisha pages|Suhama Shrine (Daito, Osaka)]][[Category:Shikinaisha|Suhama Shrine (Daito, Osaka)]][[Category:pages]][[Category:pages]] [[Category:Shinto shrine]] [[Category:Shikinai Shōsha]] [[Category:Shikinai Hiteisha]] [[Category:Shikinaisha in Kawachi Province]] [[Category:pages]] [[Category:大阪府の神社]] [[Category:大阪府の旧郷社]] [[Category:河内国の式内社]] [[Category:単立神社]] [[Category:大東市の建築物]] [[Category:大東市の歴史]]

Among them, the lantern placed at the entrance, made in 1830 (Bunsei 13), also served as a guidepost and was relocated from the nearby {{ill|lt=Higashi Kōya Kaidō|Q11528777|WD=Q11528777}} route. === Local information === ;Address * Nakagaito 2-11, {{ill|lt=Daitō City|Q386668|WD=Q386668}}, {{ill|lt=Osaka Prefecture|Q122723|WD=Q122723}} ;Access * Nearest station: About a 28-minute walk (approx. 2.2 km southeast) from JR {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Gakkentoshi Line|1=UNKNOWN}} {{ill|lt=Nozaki Station (Osaka Prefecture)|Q855914|WD=Q855914|lt=Nozaki Station}} * About a 7-minute walk after getting off at Nakagaito bus stop on the {{ill|lt=Kintetsu Bus|Q18636319|WD=Q18636319}} Shijōnawate Line === References === * “Kosha Kikō (Settsu, Kawachi, Izumi)” / Yoshinori Kunii === Related items === * {{ill|lt=List of Shikinaisha in Kawachi Province|Q11553385|WD=Q11553385}} === External links === * [https://web.archive.org/web/20181001135435/http://www.geocities.jp/engisiki/kawachi/html/040701-01.html Suhama Shrine] {{Shinto-stub}} {{Shinto horizontal}} {{DEFAULTSORT:Suhama Shrine}} == Categories == [[Category:Wikidata generated shikinaisha pages|Suhama Shrine (Daito, Osaka)]][[Category:Shikinaisha|Suhama Shrine (Daito, Osaka)]][[Category:pages]][[Category:pages]] [[Category:Shinto shrine]] [[Category:Shikinai Shōsha]] [[Category:Shikinai Hiteisha]] [[Category:Shikinaisha in Kawachi Province]] [[Category:pages]] [[Category:大阪府の神社]] [[Category:大阪府の旧郷社]] [[Category:河内国の式内社]] [[Category:単立神社]] [[Category:大東市の建築物]] [[Category:大東市の歴史]]

category|Category:Suhama-jinja (Daito, Osaka)}} [[JA:須波麻神社]] {{wikidata link|Q11664606}} == Japanese content == {{Shrine |Name= Suhama Shrine |Image=[[File:Suhama Shrine.jpg|250px]]
Distant view of the shrine grounds |Location={{ill|lt=Osaka Prefecture|Q122723|WD=Q122723|1=Osaka Prefecture}}{{ill|lt=Daitō City|Q386668|WD=Q386668|1=Daitō, Osaka}} Nakagaito 2-11 |Coordinates= {{WikidataCoord|display=inline,title}} |Enshrined deity={{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Ōkuninushi-no-Mikoto|1=Ōkuninushi|WD=Q276944}} |Shrine rank={{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Shikinaisha|1=Shikinaisha|WD=Q134917286}} (minor)
Former {{ill|lt=Gōsha|Q134917316|WD=Q134917316|1=Gō-sha}} |Established=Unknown |Main hall= |Alternative name= |Pilgrimage affiliation= |Annual festival= |Rituals= |Map = {{Location map|Japan Osaka|label=Suhama Shrine|position=bottom|width=200|mark=Shinto torii icon vermillion.svg|marksize=15|float=center|caption=}} {{maplink2|frame=yes|plain=yes|type=point|zoom=12|marker=religious-shinto|frame-align=center|frame-width=200|frame-height=200}} }} '''Suhama Shrine''' (すはまじんじゃ) is a {{ill|lt=Shinto shrine|Q845945|WD=Q845945|1=Shinto shrine}} located in Nakagaito, {{ill|lt=Daitō City|Q386668|WD=Q386668|1=Daitō, Osaka}}, {{ill|lt=Osaka Prefecture|Q122723|WD=Q122723|1=Osaka Prefecture}}. It is a {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Shikinaisha|1=Shikinaisha|WD=Q134917286}}, and its former shrine rank was {{ill|lt=Gōsha|Q134917316|WD=Q134917316|1=Gō-sha}}. === History === [[File:Kurogane-mochi at Suhama Shrine.jpg|Thumbnail|The {{ill|lt=kurogane-mochi|Q844306|WD=Q844306|1=Ilex rotunda}} tree (a {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=protected tree|1=UNKNOWN}}) within the shrine grounds]] According to tradition, the shrine was once considered identical in essence to {{ill|lt=Izumo Taisha|Q696362|WD=Q696362|1=Izumo-taisha}} and held its grand festival on October 7. Although the details of its founding are unclear, it is already listed in the Heian-period “{{ill|lt=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|Q11064932|WD=Q11064932|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō}},” and it is the only {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Shikinaisha|1=Shikinaisha|WD=Q134917286}} within Daitō City. From {{ill|lt=Sanra District|Q11632569|WD=Q11632569|1=Sasara district}} to {{ill|lt=Wakae District|Q11616589|WD=Q11616589|1=Wakae district}}, it once had parishioners from several dozen villages. In 1873 (Meiji 6), it became the {{ill|lt=Gōsha|Q134917316|WD=Q134917316|1=Gō-sha}} for eleven villages within Sanra District, and in September 1907 (Meiji 40), three shrines—{{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Ōtani Shrine|1=UNKNOWN}} in Teragawa, {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Ryūma Shrine|1=UNKNOWN}} in Ryūma, and {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Zama Shrine|1=UNKNOWN}} in Fukano—were temporarily merged into it. Today, it serves as the {{ill|lt=ubusuna-gami|Q1392097|WD=Q1392097|1=Ubusunagami}} (local tutelary deity) of Nakagaito. ;About the shrine name In ancient times, the area where this shrine stands was part of {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Kawachi Bay|1=UNKNOWN}}, which later became {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Kawachi Lagoon|1=UNKNOWN}}, then {{ill|lt=Lake Kawachi|Q11553429|WD=Q11553429|1=Kawachi Bay}}, gradually shrinking due to sediment inflow from rivers such as the {{ill|lt=Yodo River|Q1140505|WD=Q1140505|1=Yodo River}} and the {{ill|lt=Yamato River|Q9001527|WD=Q9001527|1=Yamato River}}. As this happened, settlements were gradually established, though large ponds such as {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Fukakai Pond|1=UNKNOWN}} and {{ill|lt=Fukano Pond|Q11560330|WD=Q11560330|1=Fukano Pond}} remained. It is said that the western side of this shrine once bordered such waters, and there is a theory that the shrine originally enshrined not {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Ōkuninushi-no-Mikoto|1=Ōkuninushi|WD=Q276944}} but a different water deity to ward off water-related disasters. The current name is thought to derive from “Nagisahama / Suhama” (meaning shoreline or waterside). Incidentally, the place name “Suhama” is the oldest known place name in Daitō City. === Shrine grounds === [[File:Stone lanterns at Suhama Shrine (guidepost).jpg|Thumbnail|[[Lantern|Stone lantern]] (also serving as a {{ill|lt=guidepost|Q22812402|WD=Q22812402|1=signpost}})]] In front of the shrine buildings stands a large naturally growing kurogane-mochi tree, designated as a protected tree by Daitō City. There are a total of eleven stone lanterns on the grounds. Among them, the lantern placed at the entrance, made in 1830 (Bunsei 13), also served as a guidepost and was relocated from the nearby {{ill|lt=Higashi Kōya Kaidō|Q11528777|WD=Q11528777|1=Higashi Kōya Kaidō}} route. === Local information === ;Address * Nakagaito 2-11, {{ill|lt=Daitō City|Q386668|WD=Q386668|1=Daitō, Osaka}}, {{ill|lt=Osaka Prefecture|Q122723|WD=Q122723|1=Osaka Prefecture}} ;Access * Nearest station: About a 28-minute walk (approx. 2.2 km southeast) from JR {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Gakkentoshi Line|1=UNKNOWN}} {{ill|lt=Nozaki Station (Osaka Prefecture)|Q855914|WD=Q855914|lt=Nozaki Station|1=Nozaki Station (Osaka)}} * About a 7-minute walk after getting off at Nakagaito bus stop on the {{ill|lt=Kintetsu Bus|Q18636319|WD=Q18636319|1=Kintetsu Bus}} Shijōnawate Line === References === * “Kosha Kikō (Settsu, Kawachi, Izumi)” / Yoshinori Kunii === Related items === * {{ill|lt=List of Shikinaisha in Kawachi Province|Q11553385|WD=Q11553385|1=List of Shikinaisha in Kawachi Province}} === External links === * [https://web.archive.org/web/20181001135435/http://www.geocities.jp/engisiki/kawachi/html/040701-01.html Suhama Shrine] {{Shinto-stub}} {{Shinto horizontal}} {{DEFAULTSORT:Suhama Shrine}} == Categories == [[Category:Wikidata generated shikinaisha pages|Suhama Shrine (Daito, Osaka)]][[Category:Shikinaisha|Suhama Shrine (Daito, Osaka)]][[Category:pages]][[Category:pages]] [[Category:Shinto shrine]] [[Category:Shikinai Shōsha]] [[Category:Shikinai Hiteisha]] [[Category:Shikinaisha in Kawachi Province]] [[Category:pages]] [[Category:大阪府の神社]] [[Category:大阪府の旧郷社]] [[Category:河内国の式内社]] [[Category:単立神社]] [[Category:大東市の建築物]] [[Category:大東市の歴史]]

In 1873 (Meiji 6), it became the {{ill|lt=Gōsha|Q134917316|WD=Q134917316|1=Gō-sha}} for eleven villages within Sanra District, and in September 1907 (Meiji 40), three shrines—{{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Ōtani Shrine|1=UNKNOWN}} in Teragawa, {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Ryūma Shrine|1=UNKNOWN}} in Ryūma, and {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Zama Shrine|1=UNKNOWN}} in Fukano—were temporarily merged into it. Today, it serves as the {{ill|lt=ubusuna-gami|Q1392097|WD=Q1392097|1=Ubusunagami}} (local tutelary deity) of Nakagaito. ;About the shrine name In ancient times, the area where this shrine stands was part of {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Kawachi Bay|1=UNKNOWN}}, which later became {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Kawachi Lagoon|1=UNKNOWN}}, then {{ill|lt=Lake Kawachi|Q11553429|WD=Q11553429|1=Kawachi Bay}}, gradually shrinking due to sediment inflow from rivers such as the {{ill|lt=Yodo River|Q1140505|WD=Q1140505|1=Yodo River}} and the {{ill|lt=Yamato River|Q9001527|WD=Q9001527|1=Yamato River}}. As this happened, settlements were gradually established, though large ponds such as {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Fukakai Pond|1=UNKNOWN}} and {{ill|lt=Fukano Pond|Q11560330|WD=Q11560330|1=Fukano Pond}} remained. It is said that the western side of this shrine once bordered such waters, and there is a theory that the shrine originally enshrined not {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Ōkuninushi-no-Mikoto|1=Ōkuninushi|WD=Q276944}} but a different water deity to ward off water-related disasters. The current name is thought to derive from “Nagisahama / Suhama” (meaning shoreline or waterside). Incidentally, the place name “Suhama” is the oldest known place name in Daitō City. === Shrine grounds === [[File:Stone lanterns at Suhama Shrine (guidepost).jpg|Thumbnail|[[Lantern|Stone lantern]] (also serving as a {{ill|lt=guidepost|Q22812402|WD=Q22812402|1=signpost}})]] In front of the shrine buildings stands a large naturally growing kurogane-mochi tree, designated as a protected tree by Daitō City. There are a total of eleven stone lanterns on the grounds. Among them, the lantern placed at the entrance, made in 1830 (Bunsei 13), also served as a guidepost and was relocated from the nearby {{ill|lt=Higashi Kōya Kaidō|Q11528777|WD=Q11528777|1=Higashi Kōya Kaidō}} route. === Local information === ;Address * Nakagaito 2-11, {{ill|lt=Daitō City|Q386668|WD=Q386668|1=Daitō, Osaka}}, {{ill|lt=Osaka Prefecture|Q122723|WD=Q122723|1=Osaka Prefecture}} ;Access * Nearest station: About a 28-minute walk (approx. 2.2 km southeast) from JR {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Gakkentoshi Line|1=UNKNOWN}} {{ill|lt=Nozaki Station (Osaka Prefecture)|Q855914|WD=Q855914|lt=Nozaki Station|1=Nozaki Station (Osaka)}} * About a 7-minute walk after getting off at Nakagaito bus stop on the {{ill|lt=Kintetsu Bus|Q18636319|WD=Q18636319|1=Kintetsu Bus}} Shijōnawate Line === References === * “Kosha Kikō (Settsu, Kawachi, Izumi)” / Yoshinori Kunii === Related items === * {{ill|lt=List of Shikinaisha in Kawachi Province|Q11553385|WD=Q11553385|1=List of Shikinaisha in Kawachi Province}} === External links === * [https://web.archive.org/web/20181001135435/http://www.geocities.jp/engisiki/kawachi/html/040701-01.html Suhama Shrine] {{Shinto-stub}} {{Shinto horizontal}} {{DEFAULTSORT:Suhama Shrine}} == Categories == [[Category:Wikidata generated shikinaisha pages|Suhama Shrine (Daito, Osaka)]][[Category:Shikinaisha|Suhama Shrine (Daito, Osaka)]][[Category:pages]][[Category:pages]] [[Category:Shinto shrine]] [[Category:Shikinai Shōsha]] [[Category:Shikinai Hiteisha]] [[Category:Shikinaisha in Kawachi Province]] [[Category:pages]] [[Category:大阪府の神社]] [[Category:大阪府の旧郷社]] [[Category:河内国の式内社]] [[Category:単立神社]] [[Category:大東市の建築物]] [[Category:大東市の歴史]]

It is a {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Shikinaisha|1=Shikinaisha|WD=Q134917286}}, and its former shrine rank was {{ill|lt=Gōsha|Q134917316|WD=Q134917316|1=Gō-sha}}. === History === [[File:Kurogane-mochi at Suhama Shrine.jpg|Thumbnail|The {{ill|lt=kurogane-mochi|Q844306|WD=Q844306|1=Ilex rotunda}} tree (a {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=protected tree|1=UNKNOWN}}) within the shrine grounds]] According to tradition, the shrine was once considered identical in essence to {{ill|lt=Izumo Taisha|Q696362|WD=Q696362|1=Izumo-taisha}} and held its grand festival on October 7. Although the details of its founding are unclear, it is already listed in the Heian-period “{{ill|lt=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|Q11064932|WD=Q11064932|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō}},” and it is the only {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Shikinaisha|1=Shikinaisha|WD=Q134917286}} within Daitō City. As this happened, settlements were gradually established, though large ponds such as {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Fukakai Pond|1=UNKNOWN}} and {{ill|lt=Fukano Pond|Q11560330|WD=Q11560330|1=Fukano Pond}} remained. It is said that the western side of this shrine once bordered such waters, and there is a theory that the shrine originally enshrined not {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Ōkuninushi-no-Mikoto|1=Ōkuninushi|WD=Q276944}} but a different water deity to ward off water-related disasters. The current name is thought to derive from “Nagisahama / Suhama” (meaning shoreline or waterside). Incidentally, the place name “Suhama” is the oldest known place name in Daitō City. === Shrine grounds === [[File:Stone lanterns at Suhama Shrine (guidepost).jpg|Thumbnail|[[Lantern|Stone lantern]] (also serving as a {{ill|lt=guidepost|Q22812402|WD=Q22812402|1=signpost}})]] In front of the shrine buildings stands a large naturally growing kurogane-mochi tree, designated as a protected tree by Daitō City. Distant view of the shrine grounds |Location={{ill|lt=Osaka Prefecture|Q122723|WD=Q122723|1=Osaka Prefecture}}{{ill|lt=Daitō City|Q386668|WD=Q386668|1=Daitō, Osaka}} Nakagaito 2-11 |Coordinates= {{WikidataCoord|display=inline,title}} |Enshrined deity={{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Ōkuninushi-no-Mikoto|1=Ōkuninushi|WD=Q276944}} |Shrine rank={{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Shikinaisha|1=Shikinaisha|WD=Q134917286}} (minor)
Former {{ill|lt=Gōsha|Q134917316|WD=Q134917316|1=Gō-sha}} |Established=Unknown |Main hall= |Alternative name= |Pilgrimage affiliation= |Annual festival= |Rituals= |Map = {{Location map|Japan Osaka|label=Suhama Shrine|position=bottom|width=200|mark=Shinto torii icon vermillion.svg|marksize=15|float=center|caption=}} {{maplink2|frame=yes|plain=yes|type=point|zoom=12|marker=religious-shinto|frame-align=center|frame-width=200|frame-height=200}} }} '''Suhama Shrine''' (すはまじんじゃ) is a {{ill|lt=Shinto shrine|Q845945|WD=Q845945|1=Shinto shrine}} located in Nakagaito, {{ill|lt=Daitō City|Q386668|WD=Q386668|1=Daitō, Osaka}}, {{ill|lt=Osaka Prefecture|Q122723|WD=Q122723|1=Osaka Prefecture}}. It is a {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Shikinaisha|1=Shikinaisha|WD=Q134917286}}, and its former shrine rank was {{ill|lt=Gōsha|Q134917316|WD=Q134917316|1=Gō-sha}}. === History === [[File:Kurogane-mochi at Suhama Shrine.jpg|Thumbnail|The {{ill|lt=kurogane-mochi|Q844306|WD=Q844306|1=Ilex rotunda}} tree (a {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=protected tree|1=UNKNOWN}}) within the shrine grounds]] According to tradition, the shrine was once considered identical in essence to {{ill|lt=Izumo Taisha|Q696362|WD=Q696362|1=Izumo-taisha}} and held its grand festival on October 7. Although the details of its founding are unclear, it is already listed in the Heian-period “{{ill|lt=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|Q11064932|WD=Q11064932|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō}},” and it is the only {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Shikinaisha|1=Shikinaisha|WD=Q134917286}} within Daitō City. From {{ill|lt=Sanra District|Q11632569|WD=Q11632569|1=Sasara district}} to {{ill|lt=Wakae District|Q11616589|WD=Q11616589|1=Wakae district}}, it once had parishioners from several dozen villages. In 1873 (Meiji 6), it became the {{ill|lt=Gōsha|Q134917316|WD=Q134917316|1=Gō-sha}} for eleven villages within Sanra District, and in September 1907 (Meiji 40), three shrines—{{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Ōtani Shrine|1=UNKNOWN}} in Teragawa, {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Ryūma Shrine|1=UNKNOWN}} in Ryūma, and {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Zama Shrine|1=UNKNOWN}} in Fukano—were temporarily merged into it. Today, it serves as the {{ill|lt=ubusuna-gami|Q1392097|WD=Q1392097|1=Ubusunagami}} (local tutelary deity) of Nakagaito. ;About the shrine name In ancient times, the area where this shrine stands was part of {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Kawachi Bay|1=UNKNOWN}}, which later became {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Kawachi Lagoon|1=UNKNOWN}}, then {{ill|lt=Lake Kawachi|Q11553429|WD=Q11553429|1=Kawachi Bay}}, gradually shrinking due to sediment inflow from rivers such as the {{ill|lt=Yodo River|Q1140505|WD=Q1140505|1=Yodo River}} and the {{ill|lt=Yamato River|Q9001527|WD=Q9001527|1=Yamato River}}. As this happened, settlements were gradually established, though large ponds such as {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Fukakai Pond|1=UNKNOWN}} and {{ill|lt=Fukano Pond|Q11560330|WD=Q11560330|1=Fukano Pond}} remained. It is said that the western side of this shrine once bordered such waters, and there is a theory that the shrine originally enshrined not {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Ōkuninushi-no-Mikoto|1=Ōkuninushi|WD=Q276944}} but a different water deity to ward off water-related disasters. The current name is thought to derive from “Nagisahama / Suhama” (meaning shoreline or waterside). Incidentally, the place name “Suhama” is the oldest known place name in Daitō City. === Shrine grounds === [[File:Stone lanterns at Suhama Shrine (guidepost).jpg|Thumbnail|[[Lantern|Stone lantern]] (also serving as a {{ill|lt=guidepost|Q22812402|WD=Q22812402|1=signpost}})]] In front of the shrine buildings stands a large naturally growing kurogane-mochi tree, designated as a protected tree by Daitō City. There are a total of eleven stone lanterns on the grounds. Among them, the lantern placed at the entrance, made in 1830 (Bunsei 13), also served as a guidepost and was relocated from the nearby {{ill|lt=Higashi Kōya Kaidō|Q11528777|WD=Q11528777|1=Higashi Kōya Kaidō}} route. === Local information === ;Address * Nakagaito 2-11, {{ill|lt=Daitō City|Q386668|WD=Q386668|1=Daitō, Osaka}}, {{ill|lt=Osaka Prefecture|Q122723|WD=Q122723|1=Osaka Prefecture}} ;Access * Nearest station: About a 28-minute walk (approx. 2.2 km southeast) from JR {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Gakkentoshi Line|1=UNKNOWN}} {{ill|lt=Nozaki Station (Osaka Prefecture)|Q855914|WD=Q855914|lt=Nozaki Station|1=Nozaki Station (Osaka)}} * About a 7-minute walk after getting off at Nakagaito bus stop on the {{ill|lt=Kintetsu Bus|Q18636319|WD=Q18636319|1=Kintetsu Bus}} Shijōnawate Line === References === * “Kosha Kikō (Settsu, Kawachi, Izumi)” / Yoshinori Kunii === Related items === * {{ill|lt=List of Shikinaisha in Kawachi Province|Q11553385|WD=Q11553385|1=List of Shikinaisha in Kawachi Province}} === External links === * [https://web.archive.org/web/20181001135435/http://www.geocities.jp/engisiki/kawachi/html/040701-01.html Suhama Shrine] {{Shinto-stub}} {{Shinto horizontal}} == Categories == [[Category:Wikidata generated shikinaisha pages|Suhama Shrine (Daito, Osaka)]][[Category:Shikinaisha|Suhama Shrine (Daito, Osaka)]][[Category:pages]][[Category:pages]] [[Category:Shinto shrine]] [[Category:Shikinai Shōsha]] [[Category:Shikinai Hiteisha]] [[Category:Shikinaisha in Kawachi Province]] [[Category:pages]] [[Category:大阪府の神社]] [[Category:大阪府の旧郷社]] [[Category:河内国の式内社]] [[Category:Independent shrines]] [[Category:大東市の建築物]] [[Category:大東市の歴史]]
