category|Category:Suhama-jinja (Daito, Osaka)}} [[JA:須波麻神社]] {{wikidata link|Q11664606}} == Japanese content == {{Shrine |Name= Suhama Shrine |Image=[[File:Suhama Shrine.jpg|250px]]
Distant view of the shrine grounds |Location={{ill|lt=Osaka Prefecture|Q122723|WD=Q122723|1=Osaka Prefecture}}{{ill|lt=Daitō City|Q386668|WD=Q386668|1=Daitō, Osaka}} Nakagaito 2-11 |Coordinates= {{WikidataCoord|display=inline,title}} |Enshrined deity={{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Ōkuninushi-no-Mikoto|1=Ōkuninushi|WD=Q276944}} |Shrine rank={{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Shikinaisha|1=Shikinaisha|WD=Q134917286}} (minor)
Former {{ill|lt=Gōsha|Q134917316|WD=Q134917316|1=Gō-sha}} |Established=Unknown |Main hall= |Alternative name= |Pilgrimage affiliation= |Annual festival= |Rituals= |Map = {{Location map|Japan Osaka|label=Suhama Shrine|position=bottom|width=200|mark=Shinto torii icon vermillion.svg|marksize=15|float=center|caption=}} {{maplink2|frame=yes|plain=yes|type=point|zoom=12|marker=religious-shinto|frame-align=center|frame-width=200|frame-height=200}} }} '''Suhama Shrine''' (すはまじんじゃ) is a {{ill|lt=Shinto shrine|Q845945|WD=Q845945|1=Shinto shrine}} located in Nakagaito, {{ill|lt=Daitō City|Q386668|WD=Q386668|1=Daitō, Osaka}}, {{ill|lt=Osaka Prefecture|Q122723|WD=Q122723|1=Osaka Prefecture}}. It is a {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Shikinaisha|1=Shikinaisha|WD=Q134917286}}, and its former shrine rank was {{ill|lt=Gōsha|Q134917316|WD=Q134917316|1=Gō-sha}}. === History === [[File:Kurogane-mochi at Suhama Shrine.jpg|Thumbnail|The {{ill|lt=kurogane-mochi|Q844306|WD=Q844306|1=Ilex rotunda}} tree (a {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=protected tree|1=UNKNOWN}}) within the shrine grounds]] According to tradition, the shrine was once considered identical in essence to {{ill|lt=Izumo Taisha|Q696362|WD=Q696362|1=Izumo-taisha}} and held its grand festival on October 7. Although the details of its founding are unclear, it is already listed in the Heian-period “{{ill|lt=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|Q11064932|WD=Q11064932|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō}},” and it is the only {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Shikinaisha|1=Shikinaisha|WD=Q134917286}} within Daitō City. From {{ill|lt=Sanra District|Q11632569|WD=Q11632569|1=Sasara district}} to {{ill|lt=Wakae District|Q11616589|WD=Q11616589|1=Wakae district}}, it once had parishioners from several dozen villages. In 1873 (Meiji 6), it became the {{ill|lt=Gōsha|Q134917316|WD=Q134917316|1=Gō-sha}} for eleven villages within Sanra District, and in September 1907 (Meiji 40), three shrines—{{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Ōtani Shrine|1=UNKNOWN}} in Teragawa, {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Ryūma Shrine|1=UNKNOWN}} in Ryūma, and {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Zama Shrine|1=UNKNOWN}} in Fukano—were temporarily merged into it. Today, it serves as the {{ill|lt=ubusuna-gami|Q1392097|WD=Q1392097|1=Ubusunagami}} (local tutelary deity) of Nakagaito. ;About the shrine name In ancient times, the area where this shrine stands was part of {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Kawachi Bay|1=UNKNOWN}}, which later became {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Kawachi Lagoon|1=UNKNOWN}}, then {{ill|lt=Lake Kawachi|Q11553429|WD=Q11553429|1=Kawachi Bay}}, gradually shrinking due to sediment inflow from rivers such as the {{ill|lt=Yodo River|Q1140505|WD=Q1140505|1=Yodo River}} and the {{ill|lt=Yamato River|Q9001527|WD=Q9001527|1=Yamato River}}. As this happened, settlements were gradually established, though large ponds such as {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Fukakai Pond|1=UNKNOWN}} and {{ill|lt=Fukano Pond|Q11560330|WD=Q11560330|1=Fukano Pond}} remained. It is said that the western side of this shrine once bordered such waters, and there is a theory that the shrine originally enshrined not {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Ōkuninushi-no-Mikoto|1=Ōkuninushi|WD=Q276944}} but a different water deity to ward off water-related disasters. The current name is thought to derive from “Nagisahama / Suhama” (meaning shoreline or waterside). Incidentally, the place name “Suhama” is the oldest known place name in Daitō City. === Shrine grounds === [[File:Stone lanterns at Suhama Shrine (guidepost).jpg|Thumbnail|[[Lantern|Stone lantern]] (also serving as a {{ill|lt=guidepost|Q22812402|WD=Q22812402|1=signpost}})]] In front of the shrine buildings stands a large naturally growing kurogane-mochi tree, designated as a protected tree by Daitō City. There are a total of eleven stone lanterns on the grounds. Among them, the lantern placed at the entrance, made in 1830 (Bunsei 13), also served as a guidepost and was relocated from the nearby {{ill|lt=Higashi Kōya Kaidō|Q11528777|WD=Q11528777|1=Higashi Kōya Kaidō}} route. === Local information === ;Address * Nakagaito 2-11, {{ill|lt=Daitō City|Q386668|WD=Q386668|1=Daitō, Osaka}}, {{ill|lt=Osaka Prefecture|Q122723|WD=Q122723|1=Osaka Prefecture}} ;Access * Nearest station: About a 28-minute walk (approx. 2.2 km southeast) from JR {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Gakkentoshi Line|1=UNKNOWN}} {{ill|lt=Nozaki Station (Osaka Prefecture)|Q855914|WD=Q855914|lt=Nozaki Station|1=Nozaki Station (Osaka)}} * About a 7-minute walk after getting off at Nakagaito bus stop on the {{ill|lt=Kintetsu Bus|Q18636319|WD=Q18636319|1=Kintetsu Bus}} Shijōnawate Line === References === * “Kosha Kikō (Settsu, Kawachi, Izumi)” / Yoshinori Kunii === Related items === * {{ill|lt=List of Shikinaisha in Kawachi Province|Q11553385|WD=Q11553385|1=List of Shikinaisha in Kawachi Province}} === External links === * [https://web.archive.org/web/20181001135435/http://www.geocities.jp/engisiki/kawachi/html/040701-01.html Suhama Shrine] {{Shinto-stub}} {{Shinto horizontal}} == Categories == [[Category:Wikidata generated shikinaisha pages|Suhama Shrine (Daito, Osaka)]][[Category:Shikinaisha|Suhama Shrine (Daito, Osaka)]][[Category:pages]][[Category:pages]] [[Category:Shinto shrine]] [[Category:Shikinai Shōsha]] [[Category:Shikinai Hiteisha]] [[Category:Shikinaisha in Kawachi Province]] [[Category:pages]] [[Category:大阪府の神社]] [[Category:大阪府の旧郷社]] [[Category:河内国の式内社]] [[Category:Independent shrines]] [[Category:Buildings in Daito, Osaka]] [[Category:History of Daito, Osaka]]

category|Category:Suhama-jinja (Daito, Osaka)}} [[JA:須波麻神社]] {{wikidata link|Q11664606}} == Japanese content == {{Shrine |Name= Suhama Shrine |Image=[[File:Suhama Shrine.jpg|250px]]
Distant view of the shrine grounds |Location={{ill|lt=Osaka Prefecture|Q122723|WD=Q122723|1=Osaka Prefecture}}{{ill|lt=Daitō City|Q386668|WD=Q386668|1=Daitō, Osaka}} Nakagaito 2-11 |Coordinates= {{WikidataCoord|display=inline,title}} |Enshrined deity={{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Ōkuninushi-no-Mikoto|1=Ōkuninushi|WD=Q276944}} |Shrine rank={{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Shikinaisha|1=Shikinaisha|WD=Q134917286}} (minor)
Former {{ill|lt=Gōsha|Q134917316|WD=Q134917316|1=Gō-sha}} |Established=Unknown |Main hall= |Alternative name= |Pilgrimage affiliation= |Annual festival= |Rituals= |Map = {{Location map|Japan Osaka|label=Suhama Shrine|position=bottom|width=200|mark=Shinto torii icon vermillion.svg|marksize=15|float=center|caption=}} {{maplink2|frame=yes|plain=yes|type=point|zoom=12|marker=religious-shinto|frame-align=center|frame-width=200|frame-height=200}} }} '''Suhama Shrine''' (すはまじんじゃ) is a {{ill|lt=Shinto shrine|Q845945|WD=Q845945|1=Shinto shrine}} located in Nakagaito, {{ill|lt=Daitō City|Q386668|WD=Q386668|1=Daitō, Osaka}}, {{ill|lt=Osaka Prefecture|Q122723|WD=Q122723|1=Osaka Prefecture}}. It is a {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Shikinaisha|1=Shikinaisha|WD=Q134917286}}, and its former shrine rank was {{ill|lt=Gōsha|Q134917316|WD=Q134917316|1=Gō-sha}}. === History === [[File:Kurogane-mochi at Suhama Shrine.jpg|Thumbnail|The {{ill|lt=kurogane-mochi|Q844306|WD=Q844306|1=Ilex rotunda}} tree (a {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=protected tree|1=UNKNOWN}}) within the shrine grounds]] According to tradition, the shrine was once considered identical in essence to {{ill|lt=Izumo Taisha|Q696362|WD=Q696362|1=Izumo-taisha}} and held its grand festival on October 7. Although the details of its founding are unclear, it is already listed in the Heian-period “{{ill|lt=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|Q11064932|WD=Q11064932|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō}},” and it is the only {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Shikinaisha|1=Shikinaisha|WD=Q134917286}} within Daitō City. From {{ill|lt=Sanra District|Q11632569|WD=Q11632569|1=Sasara district}} to {{ill|lt=Wakae District|Q11616589|WD=Q11616589|1=Wakae district}}, it once had parishioners from several dozen villages. In 1873 (Meiji 6), it became the {{ill|lt=Gōsha|Q134917316|WD=Q134917316|1=Gō-sha}} for eleven villages within Sanra District, and in September 1907 (Meiji 40), three shrines—{{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Ōtani Shrine|1=UNKNOWN}} in Teragawa, {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Ryūma Shrine|1=UNKNOWN}} in Ryūma, and {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Zama Shrine|1=UNKNOWN}} in Fukano—were temporarily merged into it. Today, it serves as the {{ill|lt=ubusuna-gami|Q1392097|WD=Q1392097|1=Ubusunagami}} (local tutelary deity) of Nakagaito. ;About the shrine name In ancient times, the area where this shrine stands was part of {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Kawachi Bay|1=UNKNOWN}}, which later became {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Kawachi Lagoon|1=UNKNOWN}}, then {{ill|lt=Lake Kawachi|Q11553429|WD=Q11553429|1=Kawachi Bay}}, gradually shrinking due to sediment inflow from rivers such as the {{ill|lt=Yodo River|Q1140505|WD=Q1140505|1=Yodo River}} and the {{ill|lt=Yamato River|Q9001527|WD=Q9001527|1=Yamato River}}. As this happened, settlements were gradually established, though large ponds such as {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Fukakai Pond|1=UNKNOWN}} and {{ill|lt=Fukano Pond|Q11560330|WD=Q11560330|1=Fukano Pond}} remained. It is said that the western side of this shrine once bordered such waters, and there is a theory that the shrine originally enshrined not {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Ōkuninushi-no-Mikoto|1=Ōkuninushi|WD=Q276944}} but a different water deity to ward off water-related disasters. The current name is thought to derive from “Nagisahama / Suhama” (meaning shoreline or waterside). Incidentally, the place name “Suhama” is the oldest known place name in Daitō City. === Shrine grounds === [[File:Stone lanterns at Suhama Shrine (guidepost).jpg|Thumbnail|[[Lantern|Stone lantern]] (also serving as a {{ill|lt=guidepost|Q22812402|WD=Q22812402|1=signpost}})]] In front of the shrine buildings stands a large naturally growing kurogane-mochi tree, designated as a protected tree by Daitō City. There are a total of eleven stone lanterns on the grounds. Among them, the lantern placed at the entrance, made in 1830 (Bunsei 13), also served as a guidepost and was relocated from the nearby {{ill|lt=Higashi Kōya Kaidō|Q11528777|WD=Q11528777|1=Higashi Kōya Kaidō}} route. === Local information === ;Address * Nakagaito 2-11, {{ill|lt=Daitō City|Q386668|WD=Q386668|1=Daitō, Osaka}}, {{ill|lt=Osaka Prefecture|Q122723|WD=Q122723|1=Osaka Prefecture}} ;Access * Nearest station: About a 28-minute walk (approx. 2.2 km southeast) from JR {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Gakkentoshi Line|1=UNKNOWN}} {{ill|lt=Nozaki Station (Osaka Prefecture)|Q855914|WD=Q855914|lt=Nozaki Station|1=Nozaki Station (Osaka)}} * About a 7-minute walk after getting off at Nakagaito bus stop on the {{ill|lt=Kintetsu Bus|Q18636319|WD=Q18636319|1=Kintetsu Bus}} Shijōnawate Line === References === * “Kosha Kikō (Settsu, Kawachi, Izumi)” / Yoshinori Kunii === Related items === * {{ill|lt=List of Shikinaisha in Kawachi Province|Q11553385|WD=Q11553385|1=List of Shikinaisha in Kawachi Province}} === External links === * [https://web.archive.org/web/20181001135435/http://www.geocities.jp/engisiki/kawachi/html/040701-01.html Suhama Shrine] {{Shinto-stub}} {{Shinto horizontal}} == Categories == [[Category:Wikidata generated shikinaisha pages|Suhama Shrine (Daito, Osaka)]][[Category:Shikinaisha|Suhama Shrine (Daito, Osaka)]][[Category:pages]][[Category:pages]] [[Category:Shinto shrine]] [[Category:Shikinai Shōsha]] [[Category:Shikinai Hiteisha]] [[Category:Shikinaisha in Kawachi Province]] [[Category:pages]] [[Category:Shinto shrines in Osaka Prefecture]] [[Category:Former Gō-sha in Osaka Prefecture]] [[Category:河内国の式内社]] [[Category:Independent shrines]] [[Category:Buildings in Daito, Osaka]] [[Category:History of Daito, Osaka]]

category|Category:Suhama-jinja (Daito, Osaka)}} [[JA:須波麻神社]] {{wikidata link|Q11664606}} == Japanese content == {{Shrine |Name= Suhama Shrine |Image=[[File:Suhama Shrine.jpg|250px]]
Distant view of the shrine grounds |Location={{ill|lt=Osaka Prefecture|Q122723|WD=Q122723|1=Osaka Prefecture}}{{ill|lt=Daitō City|Q386668|WD=Q386668|1=Daitō, Osaka}} Nakagaito 2-11 |Coordinates= {{WikidataCoord|display=inline,title}} |Enshrined deity={{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Ōkuninushi-no-Mikoto|1=Ōkuninushi|WD=Q276944}} |Shrine rank={{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Shikinaisha|1=Shikinaisha|WD=Q134917286}} (minor)
Former {{ill|lt=Gōsha|Q134917316|WD=Q134917316|1=Gō-sha}} |Established=Unknown |Main hall= |Alternative name= |Pilgrimage affiliation= |Annual festival= |Rituals= |Map = {{Location map|Japan Osaka|label=Suhama Shrine|position=bottom|width=200|mark=Shinto torii icon vermillion.svg|marksize=15|float=center|caption=}} {{maplink2|frame=yes|plain=yes|type=point|zoom=12|marker=religious-shinto|frame-align=center|frame-width=200|frame-height=200}} }} '''Suhama Shrine''' (すはまじんじゃ) is a {{ill|lt=Shinto shrine|Q845945|WD=Q845945|1=Shinto shrine}} located in Nakagaito, {{ill|lt=Daitō City|Q386668|WD=Q386668|1=Daitō, Osaka}}, {{ill|lt=Osaka Prefecture|Q122723|WD=Q122723|1=Osaka Prefecture}}. It is a {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Shikinaisha|1=Shikinaisha|WD=Q134917286}}, and its former shrine rank was {{ill|lt=Gōsha|Q134917316|WD=Q134917316|1=Gō-sha}}. === History === [[File:Kurogane-mochi at Suhama Shrine.jpg|Thumbnail|The {{ill|lt=kurogane-mochi|Q844306|WD=Q844306|1=Ilex rotunda}} tree (a {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=protected tree|1=UNKNOWN}}) within the shrine grounds]] According to tradition, the shrine was once considered identical in essence to {{ill|lt=Izumo Taisha|Q696362|WD=Q696362|1=Izumo-taisha}} and held its grand festival on October 7. Although the details of its founding are unclear, it is already listed in the Heian-period “{{ill|lt=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|Q11064932|WD=Q11064932|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō}},” and it is the only {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Shikinaisha|1=Shikinaisha|WD=Q134917286}} within Daitō City. From {{ill|lt=Sanra District|Q11632569|WD=Q11632569|1=Sasara district}} to {{ill|lt=Wakae District|Q11616589|WD=Q11616589|1=Wakae district}}, it once had parishioners from several dozen villages. In 1873 (Meiji 6), it became the {{ill|lt=Gōsha|Q134917316|WD=Q134917316|1=Gō-sha}} for eleven villages within Sanra District, and in September 1907 (Meiji 40), three shrines—{{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Ōtani Shrine|1=UNKNOWN}} in Teragawa, {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Ryūma Shrine|1=UNKNOWN}} in Ryūma, and {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Zama Shrine|1=UNKNOWN}} in Fukano—were temporarily merged into it. Today, it serves as the {{ill|lt=ubusuna-gami|Q1392097|WD=Q1392097|1=Ubusunagami}} (local tutelary deity) of Nakagaito. ;About the shrine name In ancient times, the area where this shrine stands was part of {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Kawachi Bay|1=UNKNOWN}}, which later became {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Kawachi Lagoon|1=UNKNOWN}}, then {{ill|lt=Lake Kawachi|Q11553429|WD=Q11553429|1=Kawachi Bay}}, gradually shrinking due to sediment inflow from rivers such as the {{ill|lt=Yodo River|Q1140505|WD=Q1140505|1=Yodo River}} and the {{ill|lt=Yamato River|Q9001527|WD=Q9001527|1=Yamato River}}. As this happened, settlements were gradually established, though large ponds such as {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Fukakai Pond|1=UNKNOWN}} and {{ill|lt=Fukano Pond|Q11560330|WD=Q11560330|1=Fukano Pond}} remained. It is said that the western side of this shrine once bordered such waters, and there is a theory that the shrine originally enshrined not {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Ōkuninushi-no-Mikoto|1=Ōkuninushi|WD=Q276944}} but a different water deity to ward off water-related disasters. The current name is thought to derive from “Nagisahama / Suhama” (meaning shoreline or waterside). Incidentally, the place name “Suhama” is the oldest known place name in Daitō City. === Shrine grounds === [[File:Stone lanterns at Suhama Shrine (guidepost).jpg|Thumbnail|[[Lantern|Stone lantern]] (also serving as a {{ill|lt=guidepost|Q22812402|WD=Q22812402|1=signpost}})]] In front of the shrine buildings stands a large naturally growing kurogane-mochi tree, designated as a protected tree by Daitō City. There are a total of eleven stone lanterns on the grounds. Among them, the lantern placed at the entrance, made in 1830 (Bunsei 13), also served as a guidepost and was relocated from the nearby {{ill|lt=Higashi Kōya Kaidō|Q11528777|WD=Q11528777|1=Higashi Kōya Kaidō}} route. === Local information === ;Address * Nakagaito 2-11, {{ill|lt=Daitō City|Q386668|WD=Q386668|1=Daitō, Osaka}}, {{ill|lt=Osaka Prefecture|Q122723|WD=Q122723|1=Osaka Prefecture}} ;Access * Nearest station: About a 28-minute walk (approx. 2.2 km southeast) from JR {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Gakkentoshi Line|1=UNKNOWN}} {{ill|lt=Nozaki Station (Osaka Prefecture)|Q855914|WD=Q855914|lt=Nozaki Station|1=Nozaki Station (Osaka)}} * About a 7-minute walk after getting off at Nakagaito bus stop on the {{ill|lt=Kintetsu Bus|Q18636319|WD=Q18636319|1=Kintetsu Bus}} Shijōnawate Line === References === * “Kosha Kikō (Settsu, Kawachi, Izumi)” / Yoshinori Kunii === Related items === * {{ill|lt=List of Shikinaisha in Kawachi Province|Q11553385|WD=Q11553385|1=List of Shikinaisha in Kawachi Province}} === External links === * [https://web.archive.org/web/20181001135435/http://www.geocities.jp/engisiki/kawachi/html/040701-01.html Suhama Shrine] {{Shinto-stub}} {{Shinto horizontal}} == Categories == [[Category:Wikidata generated shikinaisha pages|Suhama Shrine (Daito, Osaka)]][[Category:Shikinaisha|Suhama Shrine (Daito, Osaka)]][[Category:Shinto shrine]] [[Category:Shikinai Shōsha]] [[Category:Shikinai Hiteisha]] [[Category:Shikinaisha in Kawachi Province]] [[Category:Shinto shrines in Osaka Prefecture]] [[Category:Former Gō-sha in Osaka Prefecture]] [[Category:Shikinaisha in Kawachi Province]] [[Category:Independent shrines]] [[Category:Buildings in Daito, Osaka]] [[Category:History of Daito, Osaka]]

Today, it serves as the {{ill|lt=ubusuna-gami|Q1392097|WD=Q1392097|1=Ubusunagami}} (local tutelary deity) of Nakagaito. ;About the shrine name In ancient times, the area where this shrine stands was part of {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Kawachi Bay|1=UNKNOWN}}, which later became {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Kawachi Lagoon|1=UNKNOWN}}, then {{ill|lt=Lake Kawachi|Q11553429|WD=Q11553429|1=Kawachi Bay}}, gradually shrinking due to sediment inflow from rivers such as the {{ill|lt=Yodo River|Q1140505|WD=Q1140505|1=Yodo River}} and the {{ill|lt=Yamato River|Q9001527|WD=Q9001527|1=Yamato River}}. As this happened, settlements were gradually established, though large ponds such as {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Fukakai Pond|1=UNKNOWN}} and {{ill|lt=Fukano Pond|Q11560330|WD=Q11560330|1=Fukano Pond}} remained. It is said that the western side of this shrine once bordered such waters, and there is a theory that the shrine originally enshrined not {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Ōkuninushi-no-Mikoto|1=Ōkuninushi|WD=Q276944}} but a different water deity to ward off water-related disasters. The current name is thought to derive from “Nagisahama / Suhama” (meaning shoreline or waterside). Incidentally, the place name “Suhama” is the oldest known place name in Daitō City. === Shrine grounds === [[File:Stone lanterns at Suhama Shrine (guidepost).jpg|Thumbnail|[[Lantern|Stone lantern]] (also serving as a {{ill|lt=guidepost|Q22812402|WD=Q22812402|1=signpost}})]] In front of the shrine buildings stands a large naturally growing kurogane-mochi tree, designated as a protected tree by Daitō City. There are a total of eleven stone lanterns on the grounds. Among them, the lantern placed at the entrance, made in 1830 (Bunsei 13), also served as a guidepost and was relocated from the nearby {{ill|lt=Higashi Kōya Kaidō|Q11528777|WD=Q11528777|1=Higashi Kōya Kaidō}} route. === Local information === ;Address * Nakagaito 2-11, {{ill|lt=Daitō City|Q386668|WD=Q386668|1=Daitō, Osaka}}, {{ill|lt=Osaka Prefecture|Q122723|WD=Q122723|1=Osaka Prefecture}} ;Access * Nearest station: About a 28-minute walk (approx. 2.2 km southeast) from JR {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Gakkentoshi Line|1=UNKNOWN}} {{ill|lt=Nozaki Station (Osaka Prefecture)|Q855914|WD=Q855914|lt=Nozaki Station|1=Nozaki Station (Osaka)}} * About a 7-minute walk after getting off at Nakagaito bus stop on the {{ill|lt=Kintetsu Bus|Q18636319|WD=Q18636319|1=Kintetsu Bus}} Shijōnawate Line === References === * “Kosha Kikō (Settsu, Kawachi, Izumi)” / Yoshinori Kunii === Related items === * {{ill|lt=List of Shikinaisha in Kawachi Province|Q11553385|WD=Q11553385|1=List of Shikinaisha in Kawachi Province}} === External links === * [https://web.archive.org/web/20181001135435/http://www.geocities.jp/engisiki/kawachi/html/040701-01.html Suhama Shrine] {{Shinto-stub}} {{Shinto horizontal}} == Categories == [[Category:Wikidata generated shikinaisha pages|Suhama Shrine (Daito, Osaka)]][[Category:Shikinaisha|Suhama Shrine (Daito, Osaka)]][[Category:Shinto shrine]] [[Category:Shikinai Shōsha]] [[Category:Shikinai Hiteisha]] [[Category:Shikinaisha in Kawachi Province]] [[Category:Shinto shrines in Osaka Prefecture]] [[Category:Former Gō-sha in Osaka Prefecture]] [[Category:Shikinaisha in Kawachi Province]] [[Category:Independent shrines]] [[Category:Buildings and structures in Daito, Osaka]] [[Category:History of Daito, Osaka]]

It is a {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Shikinaisha|1=Shikinaisha|WD=Q134917286}}, and its former shrine rank was {{ill|lt=Gōsha|Q134917316|WD=Q134917316|1=Gō-sha}}. === History === [[File:Kurogane-mochi at Suhama Shrine.jpg|Thumbnail|The {{ill|lt=kurogane-mochi|Q844306|WD=Q844306|1=Ilex rotunda}} tree (a {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=protected tree|1=UNKNOWN}}) within the shrine grounds]] According to tradition, the shrine was once considered identical in essence to {{ill|lt=Izumo Taisha|Q696362|WD=Q696362|1=Izumo-taisha}} and held its grand festival on October 7. Although the details of its founding are unclear, it is already listed in the Heian-period “{{ill|lt=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|Q11064932|WD=Q11064932|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō}},” and it is the only {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Shikinaisha|1=Shikinaisha|WD=Q134917286}} within Daitō City. From {{ill|lt=Sanra District|Q11632569|WD=Q11632569|1=Sasara district}} to {{ill|lt=Wakae District|Q11616589|WD=Q11616589|1=Wakae district}}, it once had parishioners from several dozen villages. As this happened, settlements were gradually established, though large ponds such as {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Fukakai Pond|1=UNKNOWN}} and {{ill|lt=Fukano Pond|Q11560330|WD=Q11560330|1=Fukano Pond}} remained. It is said that the western side of this shrine once bordered such waters, and there is a theory that the shrine originally enshrined not {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Ōkuninushi-no-Mikoto|1=Ōkuninushi|WD=Q276944}} but a different water deity to ward off water-related disasters. The current name is thought to derive from “Nagisahama / Suhama” (meaning shoreline or waterside). Incidentally, the place name “Suhama” is the oldest known place name in Daitō City. === Shrine grounds === [[File:Stone lanterns at Suhama Shrine (guidepost).jpg|Thumbnail|[[Lantern|Stone lantern]] (also serving as a {{ill|lt=guidepost|Q22812402|WD=Q22812402|1=signpost}})]] In front of the shrine buildings stands a large naturally growing kurogane-mochi tree, designated as a protected tree by Daitō City. There are a total of eleven stone lanterns on the grounds. Among them, the lantern placed at the entrance, made in 1830 (Bunsei 13), also served as a guidepost and was relocated from the nearby {{ill|lt=Higashi Kōya Kaidō|Q11528777|WD=Q11528777|1=Higashi Kōya Kaidō}} route. === Local information === ;Address * Nakagaito 2-11, {{ill|lt=Daitō City|Q386668|WD=Q386668|1=Daitō, Osaka}}, {{ill|lt=Osaka Prefecture|Q122723|WD=Q122723|1=Osaka Prefecture}} ;Access * Nearest station: About a 28-minute walk (approx. 2.2 km southeast) from JR {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Gakkentoshi Line|1=UNKNOWN}} {{ill|lt=Nozaki Station (Osaka Prefecture)|Q855914|WD=Q855914|lt=Nozaki Station|1=Nozaki Station (Osaka)}} * About a 7-minute walk after getting off at Nakagaito bus stop on the {{ill|lt=Kintetsu Bus|Q18636319|WD=Q18636319|1=Kintetsu Bus}} Shijōnawate Line === References === * “Kosha Kikō (Settsu, Kawachi, Izumi)” / Yoshinori Kunii === Related items === * {{ill|lt=List of Shikinaisha in Kawachi Province|Q11553385|WD=Q11553385|1=List of Shikinaisha in Kawachi Province}} === External links === * [https://web.archive.org/web/20181001135435/http://www.geocities.jp/engisiki/kawachi/html/040701-01.html Suhama Shrine] {{Shinto-stub}} {{Shinto horizontal}} == Categories == [[Category:Wikidata generated shikinaisha pages|Suhama Shrine (Daito, Osaka)]][[Category:Shikinaisha|Suhama Shrine (Daito, Osaka)]][[Category:Shinto shrine]] [[Category:Shikinai Shōsha]] [[Category:Shikinaisha in Kawachi Province]] [[Category:Shinto shrines in Osaka Prefecture]] [[Category:Former Gō-sha in Osaka Prefecture]] [[Category:Shikinaisha in Kawachi Province]] [[Category:Independent shrines]] [[Category:Buildings and structures in Daito, Osaka]] [[Category:History of Daito, Osaka]]

Former {{ill|lt=Gōsha|Q134917316|WD=Q134917316|1=Gō-sha}} |Established=Unknown |Main hall= |Alternative name= |Pilgrimage affiliation= |Annual festival= |Rituals= |Map = {{Location map|Japan Osaka|label=Suhama Shrine|position=bottom|width=200|mark=Shinto torii icon vermillion.svg|marksize=15|float=center|caption=}} {{maplink2|frame=yes|plain=yes|type=point|zoom=12|marker=religious-shinto|frame-align=center|frame-width=200|frame-height=200}} }} '''Suhama Shrine''' (すはまじんじゃ) is a {{ill|lt=Shinto shrine|Q845945|WD=Q845945|1=Shinto shrine}} located in Nakagaito, {{ill|lt=Daitō City|Q386668|WD=Q386668|1=Daitō, Osaka}}, {{ill|lt=Osaka Prefecture|Q122723|WD=Q122723|1=Osaka Prefecture}}. It is a {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Shikinaisha|1=Shikinaisha|WD=Q134917286}}, and its former shrine rank was {{ill|lt=Gōsha|Q134917316|WD=Q134917316|1=Gō-sha}}. === History === [[File:Kurogane-mochi at Suhama Shrine.jpg|Thumbnail|The {{ill|lt=kurogane-mochi|Q844306|WD=Q844306|1=Ilex rotunda}} tree (a {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=protected tree|1=UNKNOWN}}) within the shrine grounds]] According to tradition, the shrine was once considered identical in essence to {{ill|lt=Izumo Taisha|Q696362|WD=Q696362|1=Izumo-taisha}} and held its grand festival on October 7. Although the details of its founding are unclear, it is already listed in the Heian-period “{{ill|lt=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|Q11064932|WD=Q11064932|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō}},” and it is the only {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Shikinaisha|1=Shikinaisha|WD=Q134917286}} within Daitō City. From {{ill|lt=Sanra District|Q11632569|WD=Q11632569|1=Sasara district}} to {{ill|lt=Wakae District|Q11616589|WD=Q11616589|1=Wakae district}}, it once had parishioners from several dozen villages. In 1873 (Meiji 6), it became the {{ill|lt=Gōsha|Q134917316|WD=Q134917316|1=Gō-sha}} for eleven villages within Sanra District, and in September 1907 (Meiji 40), three shrines—{{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Ōtani Shrine|1=UNKNOWN}} in Teragawa, {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Ryūma Shrine|1=UNKNOWN}} in Ryūma, and {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Zama Shrine|1=UNKNOWN}} in Fukano—were temporarily merged into it. Today, it serves as the {{ill|lt=ubusuna-gami|Q1392097|WD=Q1392097|1=Ubusunagami}} (local tutelary deity) of Nakagaito. ;About the shrine name In ancient times, the area where this shrine stands was part of {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Kawachi Bay|1=UNKNOWN}}, which later became {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Kawachi Lagoon|1=UNKNOWN}}, then {{ill|lt=Lake Kawachi|Q11553429|WD=Q11553429|1=Kawachi Bay}}, gradually shrinking due to sediment inflow from rivers such as the {{ill|lt=Yodo River|Q1140505|WD=Q1140505|1=Yodo River}} and the {{ill|lt=Yamato River|Q9001527|WD=Q9001527|1=Yamato River}}. As this happened, settlements were gradually established, though large ponds such as {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Fukakai Pond|1=UNKNOWN}} and {{ill|lt=Fukano Pond|Q11560330|WD=Q11560330|1=Fukano Pond}} remained. It is said that the western side of this shrine once bordered such waters, and there is a theory that the shrine originally enshrined not {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Ōkuninushi-no-Mikoto|1=Ōkuninushi|WD=Q276944}} but a different water deity to ward off water-related disasters. The current name is thought to derive from “Nagisahama / Suhama” (meaning shoreline or waterside). Incidentally, the place name “Suhama” is the oldest known place name in Daitō City. === Shrine grounds === [[File:Stone lanterns at Suhama Shrine (guidepost).jpg|Thumbnail|[[Lantern|Stone lantern]] (also serving as a {{ill|lt=guidepost|Q22812402|WD=Q22812402|1=signpost}})]] In front of the shrine buildings stands a large naturally growing kurogane-mochi tree, designated as a protected tree by Daitō City. There are a total of eleven stone lanterns on the grounds. Among them, the lantern placed at the entrance, made in 1830 (Bunsei 13), also served as a guidepost and was relocated from the nearby {{ill|lt=Higashi Kōya Kaidō|Q11528777|WD=Q11528777|1=Higashi Kōya Kaidō}} route. === Local information === ;Address * Nakagaito 2-11, {{ill|lt=Daitō City|Q386668|WD=Q386668|1=Daitō, Osaka}}, {{ill|lt=Osaka Prefecture|Q122723|WD=Q122723|1=Osaka Prefecture}} ;Access * Nearest station: About a 28-minute walk (approx. 2.2 km southeast) from JR {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Gakkentoshi Line|1=UNKNOWN}} {{ill|lt=Nozaki Station (Osaka Prefecture)|Q855914|WD=Q855914|lt=Nozaki Station|1=Nozaki Station (Osaka)}} * About a 7-minute walk after getting off at Nakagaito bus stop on the {{ill|lt=Kintetsu Bus|Q18636319|WD=Q18636319|1=Kintetsu Bus}} Shijōnawate Line === References === * “Kosha Kikō (Settsu, Kawachi, Izumi)” / Yoshinori Kunii === Related items === * {{ill|lt=List of Shikinaisha in Kawachi Province|Q11553385|WD=Q11553385|1=List of Shikinaisha in Kawachi Province}} === External links === * [https://web.archive.org/web/20181001135435/http://www.geocities.jp/engisiki/kawachi/html/040701-01.html Suhama Shrine] {{Shinto-stub}} {{Shinto horizontal}} == Categories == [[Category:Wikidata generated shikinaisha pages|Suhama Shrine (Daito, Osaka)]][[Category:Shikinaisha|Suhama Shrine (Daito, Osaka)]][[Category:Shikinai Shōsha]] [[Category:Shikinaisha in Kawachi Province]] [[Category:Shinto shrines in Osaka Prefecture]] [[Category:Former Gō-sha in Osaka Prefecture]] [[Category:Shikinaisha in Kawachi Province]] [[Category:Independent shrines]] [[Category:Buildings and structures in Daito, Osaka]] [[Category:History of Daito, Osaka]]
